questions.text: Which of the following represents order of ‘Horse’?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Yes, horses belong to the order Perissodactyla, which is a group of mammals characterized by an odd number of toes on their hind feet. Horses, along with other animals like zebras and rhinoceroses, are considered perissodactyls because they typically have one hoofed toe (a single large toe) on each foot. This distinguishes them from the even-toed ungulates (Artiodactyla), such as cows, deer, and pigs, which have an even number of toes on their hind feet.

The term "caballus" is often used to refer specifically to the domestic horse within the Equus ferus species. However, in the Linnaean system of biological classification, the order "Perissodactyla" is used to group horses along with other odd-toed ungulates like zebras and rhinoceroses based on shared characteristics related to their limb structure, teeth, and other anatomical features.
Ferus" is part of the species name for the wild horse. The wild horse, the Przewalski's horse, belongs to the species "Equus ferus."
Equidae is a family of horse, not order.

questions.explanation: Horses belong to the order Perissodactyla, which is a group of mammals characterized by an odd number of toes on their hind feet. Horses, along with other animals like zebras and rhinoceroses, are considered perissodactyls because they typically have one hoofed toe (a single large toe) on each foot. This distinguishes them from the even-toed ungulates (Artiodactyla), such as cows, deer, and pigs, which have an even number of toes on their hind feet.

questions.text: In case of poriferans, the spongocoel is lined with flagellated cells called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A large main opening of poriferans is called osculum.
Spongocoel is the central body cavity of the sponges. It is lined by highly specialised flagellated cells called choanocytes.
The middle region between pinachoderm and choanoderm in sponges is called mesenchyme.
Ostia are the perforated pores in sponges.

questions.explanation: Spongocoel is the central body cavity of the sponges. It is lined by highly specialised flagellated cells called choanocytes.

questions.text: Choose the correct statement.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Nociceptors are specialized receptors that primarily respond to pain stimuli, not pressure. This statement is incorrect.
Meissner's corpuscles are actually mechanoreceptors that detect light touch rather than temperature changes. This statement is incorrect.

options.text: Photoreceptors are depolarized in darkness and hyperpolarized in light
options.explanation: This statement accurately describes the function of photoreceptors. In the absence of light, photoreceptors are depolarized, while exposure to light causes them to hyperpolarize.

options.text: Receptors do not produce graded potentials
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Most sensory receptors do produce graded potentials, which are changes in the membrane potential in response to stimuli.

questions.explanation: In the dark, photoreceptors remain depolarized; light causes hyperpolarization, reducing neurotransmitter release.

questions.text: Which one of the following characteristics is not shared by birds and mammals?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Viviparity refers to the ability of an organism to give birth to live young. While some mammals are viviparous (e.g., humans, dogs, and cats), birds are not. Birds reproduce by laying eggs, which undergo external development. Therefore, viviparity is a characteristic shared by mammals but not birds.On the other hand, options B, C, and D are characteristics shared by both birds and mammals. Both birds and mammals are warm-blooded, meaning they can maintain a relatively constant body temperature. They also have an ossified endoskeleton made of bones, which provides support and protection. Additionally, both birds and mammals have lungs for breathing, enabling them to extract oxygen from the air.
Incorrect. Both birds and mammals are endothermic (warm-blooded), meaning they regulate their body temperature internally.
Incorrect. Both birds and mammals have an ossified (bony) endoskeleton, although birds have lightweight, hollow bones adapted for flight.
Incorrect. Both birds and mammals breathe using lungs, though birds have a more advanced air sac system for efficient respiration.

Additionally, both birds and mammals have lungs for breathing, enabling them to extract oxygen from the air.

options.text: Possess a mouth with an upper and a lower jaw - Chordata
options.explanation: Phylum Chordata includes both jawless vertebrates (Agnatha) and jawed vertebrates (Gnathostomata).

Duck billed platypus and spiny anteater are oviparous mammals.

questions.text: Which of the following features is not present in the Phylum Arthropoda?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Parapodia are flattened, fleshy, vertical flap like outgrowths of body wall found in annelids on lateral sides of trunk segments. These are hollow structures enclosing coelom which is continuous with that of trunk segments. These serve the dual purpose of locomotion and respiration.
Arthropods possess jointed appendages, which are segmented and hinged structures that allow for versatile and flexible movement, such as walking, grasping, and feeding.
Arthropods possess a chitinous exoskeleton, a tough, protective outer covering made of chitin, which provides support, protection, and serves as a surface for muscle attachment.
Arthropods possess a chitinous exoskeleton, a tough, protective outer covering made of chitin, which provides support, protection, and serves as a surface for muscle attachment.

questions.explanation: Parapodia are flattened, fleshy, vertical flap like outgrowths of body wall found in annelids on lateral sides of trunk segments. These are hollow structures enclosing coelom which is continuous with that of trunk segments. These serve the dual purpose of locomotion and respiration.

questions.text: Body having a meshwork of cells, internal cavities lined with food filtering flagellated cells and indirect development are the characteristics of which Phylum?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mollusca have a hard shell, a mantle, and a foot. Have true tissues and organs, not a meshwork of cells. No flagellated collar cells. Have direct or indirect development but not the described body plan.
Protozoa are single-celled organisms. No internal cavity or body made of cells — just one single cell.

Coelenterata have a body with two layers of cells and a central cavity. Have tissue level of organization and a gastrovascular cavity, not a system of canals and pores.Their body wall has cnidocytes (stinging cells), not flagellated collar cells.

Porifera, commonly known as sponges, exhibit the described characteristics. Sponges have a meshwork of cells called choanocytes that line their internal cavities. These choanocytes have flagella that create water currents to filter and capture food particles. Sponges also undergo indirect development, meaning they go through larval stages before reaching their adult form.

questions.explanation: The phylum that has a body having a meshwork of cells, internal cavities lined with food-filtering flagellated cells, and indirect development is Porifera. Porifera, also known as sponges, are the simplest multicellular animals. They are characterized by a meshwork of cells called the spongocoel, which is lined with flagellated cells called choanocytes. The choanocytes create a water current that brings food and oxygen into the sponge and carries away waste. Porifera also has an indirect development, meaning that the young form is different from the adult form.

questions.text: Metagenesis refers to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Occurrence of a drastic change in form during postembryonic development
options.explanation: The occurrence of a drastic change in form during postembryonic development is known as "metamorphosis."

options.text: Presence of a segmented body and parthenogenetic mode of reproduction
options.explanation: The presence of a segmented body and a parthenogenetic mode of reproduction is characteristic of some arthropods, like certain species of ants and bees. Parthenogenesis is a form of asexual reproduction where offspring develop from unfertilized eggs. This combination of features is particularly common in some Hymenoptera species.
The presence of different morphic forms within a single species is often referred to as "polymorphism."

options.text: Alternation of generation between asexual and sexual phases of an organism
options.explanation: An alternation of generation between asexual and sexual phases of an organism is referred to as metagenesis. E.g. in Obelia (a coelenterate), polyps reproduce asexually and medusae reproduce sexually.

questions.explanation: An alternation of generation between asexual and sexual phases of an organism is referred to as metagenesis. E.g. in Obelia (a coelenterate), polyps reproduce asexually and medusae reproduce sexually.

questions.text: A jawless fish lays eggs in fresh water and it has ammocoetes larvae after metamorphosis. This fish return to the ocean as _:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Hagfish, including those in the Neomyxine genus, typically undergo a larval stage after hatching, which eventually undergoes metamorphosis to become a juvenile hagfish.

Petromyzon (Lamprey) belongs to the Class Cyclostomata of Phylum Chordata. It is a jawless fish which lays eggs in fresh water. The eggs hatch in about 3 weeks into minute transparent larvae called ammocoetes. After metamorphosis, the young lam preys swim down to the sea where they remain for 3 or 4 years before reaching maturity, when they once again migrate to streams or rivers to spawn and die. Gonads become mature when adults return to rivers for spawning.
Hagfish, including those in the genus Eptatretus, typically undergo a larval stage after hatching, which eventually undergoes metamorphosis to become a juvenile hagfish.

Myxine is another genus of hagfish, like Eptatretus, and their life cycle is similar. After hatching, Myxine larvae also go through a metamorphosis from a larval stage known as a "yolk-sac larva" to become juvenile hagfish.

questions.explanation: Petromyzon (Lamprey) belongs to the Class Cyclostomata of Phylum Chordata. It is a jawless fish which lays eggs in fresh water. The eggs hatch in about 3 weeks into minute transparent larvae called ammocoetes. After metamorphosis, the young lam preys swim down to the sea where they remain for 3 or 4 years before reaching maturity, when they once again migrate to streams or rivers to spawn and die. Gonads become mature when adults return to rivers for spawning.

questions.text: Which of the following endoparasites of humans does show viviparity?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Trichinella spiralis is a minute nematode parasite that shows viviparity i.e., produces live youngs (larvae) not eggs. The adults of T. spiralis live in the human small intestine, where the females release large numbers of larvae. These larvae bore through the intestine and can cause trichinosis or trichiniasis which has symptoms like diarrohea, nausea, vertigo, pain in limbs and fever etc. Humans get infected after eating imperfectly cooked meat infected with the parasite’s larval cysts.
Ascaris lumbricoides, which is a type of roundworm, is oviparous. This means that it lays eggs, and the young (larvae) develop outside the mother's body.
Taenia, a genus of tapeworms, is typically oviparous, which means they lay eggs.
Enterobius vermicularis, commonly known as the human pinworm, is oviparous. This parasitic worm lives in the large intestine of humans and lays eggs in the perianal region.

questions.explanation: Trichinella spiralis is a minute nematode parasite that shows viviparity i.e., produces live young (larvae), not eggs. The adults of T. spiralis live in the human small intestine, where the females release large numbers of larvae. These larvae bore through the intestine and can cause trichinosis or trichiniasis, which has symptoms like diarrhoea, nausea, vertigo, pain in limbs and fever, etc. Humans get infected after eating imperfectly cooked meat infected with the parasite’s larval cysts.

questions.text: Which of the following animals is not viviparous?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Duckbilled platypus is an egg laying mammal. It is found in the rivers in eastern Australia and Tasmania. It is a beaver like monotreme about 5060 cm long and well adapted to live in water. Usually, two eggs are laid at a time. The female curls around them for incubation and remains inactive for about two weeks. Newly hatched young ones are very immature, naked, blind and each is 2.5 cm long.
Whales are viviparous, which means they give birth to live offspring rather than laying eggs.
Bats are viviparous, which means they give birth to live offspring rather than laying eggs.
Elephants are viviparous, which means they give birth to live offspring rather than laying eggs.

questions.explanation: Platypus is a monotreme, a special mammal that lays eggs instead of giving birth to live young. Whales, bats (flying fox), and elephants are all viviparous mammals that give birth to live young.

questions.text: Which of the following characteristics is mainly responsible for the diversification of insects on land?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The main characteristic responsible for the diversification of insects on land is the exoskeleton. The exoskeleton is a hard, protective covering that surrounds the insect's body. It is made of chitin, a tough, fibrous substance that helps to prevent the insect from drying out and from being damaged by predators. The exoskeleton also provides a place for muscles to attach, which allows insects to move quickly and easily.
Eyes allow insects to see their surroundings, which is important for finding food and avoiding predators.
Segmentation allows insects to move their body parts independently, which gives them more flexibility and maneuverability.
Bilateral symmetry means that the insect's body is divided into two mirror-image halves, which helps to protect the insect from injury.

questions.explanation: The exoskeleton, or outer skeleton, is a key characteristic of insects that has contributed significantly to their diversification on land. The exoskeleton provides structural support, protection, and resistance to desiccation, allowing insects to thrive in terrestrial environments. It also serves as a site for muscle attachment, enabling efficient movement and locomotion. The rigid exoskeleton of insects provides advantages such as protection against predators and the ability to exploit a wide range of ecological niches. Additionally, the exoskeleton undergoes molting, allowing for growth and development throughout an insect's life cycle. The unique properties and adaptability of the exoskeleton have played a major role in the successful colonization and diversification of insects on land.

questions.text: Select the taxonomy mentioned that represents both marine and fresh water species.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Echinoderms are exclusively marine animals that live in salty ocean water. They include organisms such as starfish, sea urchins, and sea cucumbers. Their body structure and water vascular system are specially adapted for marine environments, which is why they cannot survive in freshwater. Hence, echinoderms are found only in the sea and not in any freshwater habitats.

Ctenophores, commonly known as comb jellies, are also exclusively marine organisms. They are gelatinous, transparent animals that float in ocean waters and use cilia for movement. These animals are beautifully bioluminescent but cannot tolerate freshwater conditions. Therefore, ctenophores are restricted to marine environments only.

Members of the subphylum Cephalochordata, such as Amphioxus (Branchiostoma), are small, fish-like chordates that live buried in marine sand. They are found in shallow sea waters and have no representatives in freshwater habitats. Their structural and physiological features are specialized for life in marine conditions, so this group is also purely marine.

Cnidarians are the sac-like animals which are aquatic. They are mostly marine except a few like hydra which are fresh water. They are the simplest organisms that have attained a tissue level of organization.

questions.explanation: Cnidarians are the sac-like animals which are aquatic. They are mostly marine except a few like hydra which are fresh water. They are the simplest organisms that have attained a tissue level of organization. Members of Ctenophora, Cephalochordata and Echinodermata are exclusively marine.

questions.text: Which one of the following living organisms completely lacks a cell wall?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The cell wall of cyanobacteria is commonly referred to as the peptidoglycan layer.
Gorgonia (sea fan) is an animal belonging to phylum Coelenterata. All animals lack a cell wall.

Saccharomyces has a cell wall. The cell wall of Saccharomyces yeast is primarily composed of a combination of β-glucans and mannoproteins, providing structural support and interacting with the environment.
Blue-green algae (cyanobacteria) have cell walls. The cell wall of cyanobacteria typically contains peptidoglycan and other components, serving to provide structural support and protection.

questions.explanation: Gorgonia (sea fan) is an animal belonging to phylum Coelenterata. All animals lack a cell wall. Gorgonia is a genus of soft corals, sea fans in the family Gorgoniidae.

questions.text: Planaria possesses a high capacity of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Metamorphosis is a biological process where an organism undergoes significant changes in its body structure and physiology as it develops from one life stage to another. but planaria do not undergo metamorphosis like some other animals.

Planaria possesses high degree of regeneration. Both epimorphosis (in which the missing parts are formed) and morphallaxis (in which the whole body can be regenerated from a fragment of the body) occur.
Planaria do not exhibit alternation of generations. Alternation of generations is a life cycle pattern found in some organisms, like certain plants and algae.

Planaria do not exhibit bioluminescence. Bioluminescence is the production and emission of light by living organisms, often due to chemical reactions involving light-emitting molecules called luciferins.

questions.explanation: Planaria possesses high degree of regeneration. Both epimorphosis (in which the missing parts are formed) and morphallaxis (in which the whole body can be regenerated from a fragment of the body) occur.

questions.text: A marine cartilaginous fish that can produce electric current is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Pristis, commonly known as sawfish, do not produce electric currents.

Torpedo is a bottomliving marine fish, discharging electricity which is sufficient to stun preys such as small fishes, etc. A pair of electric organs are situated on the dorsal side of the trunk region. Infact, the electric organs are modified lateral muscle plates innervated by the cranial nerves.

Some trygons produce electric currents, such as: torpedo. But we cannot mark this option right because it includes all trygons, and only some trygons like torpedo can produce electric currents not all.
Scoliodon, commonly known as the spadenose shark or the dogfish shark, does not produce electric current.

Infact, the electric organs are modified lateral muscle plates innervated by the cranial nerves.

questions.text: Which of the following are correctly matched with respect to their taxonomic classification?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Housefly, butterfly, Insecta tsetse fly, silverfish
options.explanation: Yes, houseflies, butterflies, tsetse flies, and silverfish all belong to the same phylum in the animal kingdom, which is Arthropoda. The phylum Arthropoda is characterized by having jointed legs and a segmented exoskeleton, and it includes a vast diversity of invertebrate animals, including insects (like houseflies, butterflies, and tsetse flies) and other arthropods (like silverfish).

options.text: Spiny anteater, sea urchin, sea cucumber
options.explanation: Spiny ant eater (Echidna) is a prototherian mammal whereas, sea urchins and sea cucumbers are echinoderms.

options.text: Flying fish, Cuttle fish, Pisces silver fish

Centipede is class chilopoda, Millipede is class diplopoda and Scorpion and Spider are class arachnida of Phylum Arthropoda.

options.explanation: Puffer fish and flying fish (Exocoetus) are examples of superclass Pisces.
Puffer fish and flying fish (Exocoetus) are examples of superclass Pisces.
Cuttle fish (Sepia) belongs to Mollusca.
Phylum Arthropoda is the largest phylum of Animalia which includes insects. Examples include Apis, silkworm, Laccifer, silver fish (Lepisma), locust, etc.

questions.text: The characteristics of Class Reptilia are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Body covered with moist skin which is devoid of scales, the ear is represented by a tympanum, alimentary canal, urinary and reproductive tracts open into a common cloaca
options.explanation: These characteristics describe Class Amphibia, which are adapted to both aquatic and terrestrial environments but have moist skin without scales.

options.text: Fresh water animals with bony endoskeleton, airbladder to regulate buoyancy
options.explanation: These are the characteristics of Class Osteichthyes, or bony fish, which primarily live in aquatic environments and have a bony endoskeleton and an air bladder.

options.text: Marine animals with cartilaginous endoskeleton, body covered with placoid scales
options.explanation: These characteristics describe Class Chondrichthyes, which includes cartilaginous fish such as sharks and rays, known for their marine habitat and placoid scales.

options.text: Body covered with dry and cornified skin, scales over the body are epidermal, they do not have external ears
options.explanation: These are the defining characteristics of Class Reptilia, which are adapted for terrestrial life with dry, cornified skin and lack external ears.

questions.explanation: These are the defining characteristics of Class Reptilia, which are adapted for terrestrial life with dry, cornified skin and lack external ears.

questions.text: Which one of the following groups of animals reproduces only by sexual means?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cnidaria, such as jellyfish, corals, and sea anemones, can reproduce both sexually and asexually. For example, the medusa stage typically engages in sexual reproduction, while the polyp stage often reproduces asexually through budding.

Porifera, or sponges, are capable of both sexual and asexual reproduction. Asexual reproduction occurs through budding or fragmentation, while sexual reproduction occurs with the production of gametes as they are hermaphroditic.

Protozoa exhibit both sexual and asexual reproduction. Methods of asexual reproduction include binary fission and budding, while sexual reproduction can involve processes like syngamy and conjugation, depending on the species.

Ctenophores, also known as comb jellies, reproduce exclusively through sexual means. They are typically hermaphroditic, with external fertilization being the norm. Unlike the other groups mentioned, ctenophores do not reproduce asexually.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Ctenophora. Ctenophores, or comb jellies, reproduce solely through sexual reproduction and lack any form of asexual reproduction. They are monoecious, meaning individual organisms have both male and female reproductive organs, allowing for sexual reproduction to take place externally. In contrast, Cnidaria can reproduce both sexually (in the medusa stage) and asexually (in the polyp stage). Porifera, or sponges, can also reproduce both ways, with asexual reproduction occurring through mechanisms like budding. Similarly, Protozoa can reproduce both sexually and asexually, using methods such as binary fission and conjugation depending on the species and environmental conditions.

questions.text: Which one of the following animals is correctly matched with its one characteristic and the taxon?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Millipedes belong to the class Diplopoda within the phylum Arthropoda, not Arachnida. Although millipedes possess a ventral nerve cord, Arachnida is a different class that includes creatures like spiders and scorpions.

This option is incorrect. Sea anemones are part of the phylum Cnidaria, which is characterized by being diploblastic. They have two germ layers rather than three, distinguishing them from triploblastic animals.

options.text: Silverfish - Pectoral and Pelvic Fins - Chordata
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Silverfish are insects under the class Insecta within the phylum Arthropoda. They lack pectoral and pelvic fins, which are typical of some aquatic chordates like fish.
This option is correct. The duckbilled platypus is a distinctive mammal under the order Monotremata in the class Mammalia. It is oviparous, meaning it lays eggs, a rare trait among mammals.

questions.explanation: The duckbilled platypus is a distinctive mammal under the order Monotremata in the class Mammalia. It is oviparous, meaning it lays eggs, a rare trait among mammals.

questions.text: Sharks and dogfishes differ from skates and rays because:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Their gill slits are ventrally placed
options.explanation: The gill slits of sharks and dogfishes are laterally placed, meaning they are located on the sides of their bodies, unlike in skates and rays where gill slits can be found ventrally.

options.text: Their head and trunk are widened considerably
options.explanation: Sharks and dogfishes do not have a considerably widened head and trunk. Instead, they have a streamlined body shape for efficient swimming, whereas skates and rays have a flattened body structure.

options.text: There is distinct demarcation between body and tail
options.explanation: Sharks and dogfishes have a streamlined body with no distinct separation between the body and tail, unlike some other animal groups. This is not a distinguishing feature between them and skates and rays.

options.text: Their pectoral fins distinctly marked off from cylindrical bodies
options.explanation: Sharks and dogfishes have distinct pectoral fins that are clearly separated from their cylindrical bodies, whereas skates and rays have pectoral fins that are fused to the body, giving them a wing-like appearance.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that sharks and dogfishes have their pectoral fins distinctly marked off from their cylindrical bodies. This is in contrast to skates and rays, whose pectoral fins are fused with the body, creating a wing-like structure. This distinction is crucial in differentiating these groups within the Chondrichthyes class. The other options are incorrect because they describe features that do not accurately differentiate sharks and dogfishes from skates and rays.

questions.text: Which one of the following is one of the paths followed by air or oxygen during respiration in the adult male Periplaneta Americana as it enters the animal body?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Spiracle in metathorax, trachea, tracheloes, oxygen diffuses into cells
options.explanation: Cockroaches utilize a tracheal system for respiration. Air enters through spiracles located on the thorax and abdomen, with the metathoracic spiracle being a key point of entry. The air travels through tracheae and tracheoles, facilitating direct oxygen diffusion into cells.

options.text: Mouth, bronchial tube, trachea, oxygen enters cells
options.explanation: Unlike mammals, cockroaches do not use a mouth or bronchial tubes for respiration. Their system consists of spiracles, tracheae, and tracheoles, which bypasses the need for such structures.

options.text: Spiracles in prothorax, tracheoles, trachea, oxygen diffuses into cells
options.explanation: While spiracles are essential for respiration, the primary spiracles for air entry in Periplaneta americana are located in the metathorax, not the prothorax.
The hypopharynx and mouth are involved in feeding, not respiration, in cockroaches. Their respiration relies on the tracheal system that completely bypasses these structures.

questions.explanation: The respiratory system of Periplaneta americana, or the American cockroach, is distinct from that of mammals. It consists of a network of tracheae and tracheoles through which air is distributed directly to the cells. Air enters the body through spiracles, small openings on the thorax and abdomen. Specifically, the metathoracic spiracle is one of the primary entry points in the thorax. This system compensates for the cockroach's less efficient circulatory system by directly delivering oxygen to tissues. Other options incorrectly describe a mammalian respiratory path or incorrectly locate the spiracles.

questions.text: Pheretima and its close relatives derive nourishment from:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Roundworms, not earthworms like Pheretima, may feed on roots such as those of sugarcane. Earthworms primarily consume organic matter in the soil.

options.text: Decaying fallen leaves and soil organic matter
options.explanation: Pheretima, a genus of earthworms, primarily feeds on decaying organic matter found in the soil, including fallen leaves and other plant debris. This makes them crucial for nutrient cycling and soil aeration.
Earthworms do not primarily consume soil insects. They focus on decomposing organic material, which helps in forming nutrient-rich soil.

options.text: Small pieces of fresh fallen leaves of maize, etc
options.explanation: While earthworms may encounter fresh leaves, their diet mainly consists of decaying organic matter, as it is easier to digest and more nutrient-rich.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that Pheretima and its close relatives derive nourishment from decaying fallen leaves and soil organic matter. Earthworms, including those in the Pheretima genus, are detritivores; they play a role in breaking down and recycling dead plant material, thus improving soil structure and fertility. Other options such as sugarcane roots, soil insects, and fresh leaves do not accurately represent their diet. Earthworms are not equipped to consume live plant roots or soil insects, and fresh leaves are not their primary food source as they prefer decomposed material for easier digestion.

questions.text: In which one of the following options are the genus name, its two characters and its phylum not correctly matched?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Pila (i) Body segmented Mollusca (ii) Mouth with radula
options.explanation: Incorrect: Pila is a genus within the phylum Mollusca, but molluscs have unsegmented bodies. The radula is correctly associated with Pila's mouth, but the body segmentation is incorrect.

options.text: Asterias (i) Spiny skinned Echinodermata (ii) Water vascular system
options.explanation: Correct: Asterias, or starfish, are echinoderms that have spiny skin and a water vascular system, which are characteristic of the phylum Echinodermata.

options.text: Sycon (i) Pore bearing Porifera (ii) Canal system
options.explanation: Correct: Sycon is a genus within the phylum Porifera. They are known for their pore-bearing bodies and canal systems, which are typical features of sponges.

options.text: Periplaneta (i) Jointed appendages Arthropoda (ii) Chitinous exoskeleton
options.explanation: Correct: Periplaneta, commonly known as cockroaches, belong to the phylum Arthropoda. They have jointed appendages and a chitinous exoskeleton, both of which are distinctive characteristics of arthropods.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Pila is correctly classified under the phylum Mollusca, but the claim that its body is segmented is incorrect, as molluscs typically have unsegmented bodies. The radula is a characteristic feature of Pila, but the segmentation trait does not apply. In contrast, other options accurately match the genus, traits, and phylum: Asterias has spiny skin and a water vascular system typical of Echinodermata; Sycon has pore-bearing and canal system traits of Porifera; and Periplaneta has jointed appendages and a chitinous exoskeleton characteristic of Arthropoda.

questions.text: Which one of the following pairs of animals are similar to each other pertaining to the feature stated against them?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Pteropus genus, are mammals and typically give birth to live young, which is a reproductive method known as viviparity. Ornithorhynchus, on the other hand, is the scientific name for the platypus. Platypuses are unique monotremes, a group of egg-laying mammals. So, this combination is not correct.

options.text: Garden lizard and crocodile - Three chambered heart
options.explanation: Garden lizard is three chambered hearted, but crocodile has a heart of four chambers. So, this combination is not correct.

Ascaris is a genus of roundworms (nematodes), and they do not exhibit segmentation. Roundworms are typically unsegmented and have a cylindrical body shape. Ancylostoma is another genus of roundworms, commonly known as hookworms. Like Ascaris, they are also unsegmented.

options.text: Sea horse and flying fish - Cold blooded (poikilothermal)
options.explanation: Sea horse (Hippocampus) and flying fish (Exocoetus) belong to class osteichthyes of super class pisces. They have two chambered hearts (one auricle and one ventricle) and are cold blooded animals. So, this option is correct.

questions.explanation: Sea horse (Hippocampus) and flying fish (Exocoetus) belong to class osteichthyes of super class pisces. They have two chambered hearts (one auricle and one ventricle) and are cold blooded animals.

questions.text: Which one of the following categories of animals is correctly described with no exception in it?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: All reptiles possess scales, have a three chambered heart and are cold blooded
options.explanation: The heart is generally 3 chambered in reptiles but in the crocodile it is 4 chambered.

options.text: All bony fishes have four pairs of gills and an operculum on each side
options.explanation: All bony fishes do have four pairs of gills and an operculum on each side of their bodies. The operculum is a protective bony plate that covers and helps protect the delicate gill filaments. This arrangement allows bony fishes to extract oxygen from the water as it flows over their gills and to expel carbon dioxide.

options.text: All sponges are marine and have collared cells
options.explanation: Sponges are generally marine and have collared cells but few fresh water forms can also be seen like Spongilla.

options.text: All mammals are viviparous and possess diaphragm for breathing
options.explanation: All mammals are viviparous (giving birth to young ones) with an oviparous (egg laying) exception: Ornithorhynchus (platypus).

questions.explanation: All bony fishes do have four pairs of gills and an operculum on each side of their bodies. The operculum is a protective bony plate that covers and helps protect the delicate gill filaments. This arrangement allows bony fishes to extract oxygen from the water as it flows over their gills and to expel carbon dioxide. The heart is generally 3 chambered in reptiles but in the crocodile it is 4 chambered. Sponges are generally marine and have collared cells but few fresh water forms can also be seen like Spongilla. All mammals are viviparous (giving birth to young ones) with an oviparous (egg laying) exception: Ornithorhynchus (platypus).

questions.text: What will you look for to identify the sex of the following?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Female Ascaris - sharply curved posterior end
options.explanation: Tail end is straight in female Ascaris, while tail end is curved towards ventral in male Ascaris.

options.text: Male frog - a copulatory pad on the first digit of the hind limb
options.explanation: The forelimbs in both frogs (male and female) bear small articular pads dorsally at the joints of digit, but the males possess a special nuptial, copulatory pad on ventral side of the first finger of each forelimb. Copulatory pads appear merely as rough patches, but during breeding season, they become thick and sticky. In copulation, the male strongly grips a female under her armpits by means of these pads.
Anal cerci is present in both male and female cockroach, while anal style is present only in the male cockroach.

options.text: Male shark - claspers borne on pelvic fins
options.explanation: Claspers are modified inner edges of pelvic fins in male sharks.Female sharks do not have claspers.

questions.explanation: Tail end is straight in female Ascaris, while tail end is curved towards ventral in male Ascaris. Anal cerci is present in both male and female cockroach, while anal style is present only in the male cockroach. The forelimbs in both frogs (male and female) bear small articular pads dorsally at the joints of digit, but the males possess a special nuptial, copulatory pad on ventral side of the first finger of each forelimb. Copulatory pads appear merely as rough patches, but during breeding season, they become thick and sticky. In copulation, the male strongly grips a female under her armpits by means of these pads. Claspers are modified inner edges of pelvic fins in male sharks.

questions.text: In which one of the following the genus name, its two characters and its class/phylum are correctly matched?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Ascaris (i) Body segmented Annelida (ii) Males and females distinct
options.explanation: Ascaris is member of phylum Nematoda, which are round worms. They do not have segmented body. In ascaris female is longer than male.

options.text: Salamandra (i) A tympanum represents ear (ii) Fertilization is external Amphibia
options.explanation: Salamanders do not have tympanum, although they have greatly reduced middle ears and fertilization is usually internal in them.
Pteropus is member of class mammalia. They are viviparous. A mantle of golden hair covers the head, neck and shoulders in Pteropus.
None of them are correct.

questions.explanation: None of the options is correct. Ascaris is member of phylum Nematoda, which are round worms. They do not have segmented body. In ascaris female is longer than male. Salamandra is member of amphibia. Salamanders do not have tympanum, although they have greatly reduced middle ears and fertilization is usually internal in them. Pteropus is member of class mammalia. They are viviparous. A mantle of golden hair covers the head, neck and shoulders in Pteropus.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is totally wrong about the occurrence of notochord, while the other three are correct?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: It is present only in larval tail in ascidian
options.explanation: In Ascidia (urochordata), notochord is present only in larval tail

options.text: It is replaced by a vertebral column in adult frog
options.explanation: Yes, notochord is replaced by a vertebral column in adult frog.

options.text: It is absent throughout life in humans from the very beginning
options.explanation: Humans are mammals which are chordates. Phylum chordata includes animals which possess a notochord either throughout or during early embryonic life. This option is correct.

options.text: It is present throughoutlife in Amphioxus
options.explanation: In Amphioxus (cephalochordata), it extends from head to tail region and is persistent throughout their life.

questions.explanation: Humans are mammals which are chordates. Phylum chordata includes animals which possess a notochord either throughout or during early embryonic life. In Ascidia (urochordata), notochord is present only in larval tail while in Amphioxus (cephalochordata), it extends from head to tail region and is persistent throughout their life. It is replaced by a vertebral column in adult frog.

questions.text: The figures (A – D) show four animals. Select the correct option with respect to a common characteristic of two of these animals.
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: (A) and (D) respire mainly through body wall
options.explanation: (D) respire through lungs or gills
(C) shows bilateral symmetry.

options.text: (A and (B) have cnidoblasts for self defence
options.explanation: (A) do not have cnidoblasts.

The animals which possess true coelom are called eucoelomates or coelomates. The true coelom is a body cavity which arises as a cavity in embryonic mesoderm. True coelom is of two types; schizocoelom (schizocoel) and enterocoelom (enterocoel). Schizocoelom develops as a split in the mesoderm sheet. It is found in annelids, arthropods, molluscs. In enterocoelom, mesoderm arises from the wall of the embryonic cut of enteron as hollow outgrowths. It occurs in echinoderms and chordates.

questions.explanation: The animals which possess true coelom are called eucoelomates or coelomates. The true coelom is a body cavity which arises as a cavity in embryonic mesoderm. True coelom is of two types; schizocoelom (schizocoel) and enterocoelom (enterocoel). Schizocoelom develops as a split in the mesoderm sheet. It is found in annelids, arthropods, molluscs. In enterocoelom, mesoderm arises from the wall of the embryonic cut of enteron as hollow outgrowths. It occurs in echinoderms and chordates.

questions.text: Ureters act as urinogenital ducts in _ .
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In human females, only urethra acts as urinogenital duct, not ureter.The ureter carries only urine from kidney to bladder.The sperms travel through a different tube — the vas deferens (not the ureter).Hence, ureter ≠ urinogenital duct in humans.
Human females do not have urinogenital ducts.The genital system (ovaries, oviducts, uterus) is separate from the excretory system.
Only male frogs have urinogenital ducts.Females have separate oviducts for eggs, not combined with the urinary system.

In male frogs, two ureters act as the urinogenital duct which opens into the cloaca. They run backwards from the kidneys and open into the cloaca. In females, the ureters carry urine alone, while in males both sperm and urine are carried. Hence, they are called urinogenital ducts.

questions.explanation: In male frogs, two ureters act as the urinogenital duct which opens into the cloaca. They run backwards from the kidneys and open into the cloaca. In females, the ureters carry urine alone, while in males both sperm and urine are carried. Hence, they are called urinogenital ducts.

questions.text: All animals having a single opening to the outside that serves both as mouth as well as anus except?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Octopuses do not have a single external opening for both ingestion and egestion. Octopuses have a more complex digestive system compared to some other animals. They have separate openings for ingestion and egestion.
Asterias, commonly known as starfish or sea stars, typically have a single external opening on the underside of their body for both ingestion and egestion.

Ascidians, also known as sea squirts or tunicates, have a single opening for both ingestion and excretion, which is known as the siphon. The siphon serves as both the inlet and outlet for water in their filter-feeding system.

In Fasciola (flatworms) the body has a single cavity with one opening to the outside. The single opening functions as both mouth for ingestion (intake of food) and anus for egestion (undigested food is passed out). It is called blind sac plan.

questions.explanation: Octopuses do not have a single external opening for both ingestion and egestion. Octopuses have a more complex digestive system compared to some other animals. They have separate openings for ingestion and egestion.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements about all the four of Spongilla, leech, dolphin and penguin is correct?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Penguin is homeothermic while the remaining three are poikilothermic
options.explanation: Penguins are indeed homeothermic, also known as endothermic. Sponges and leech are poikilothermic or ectothermic animals. Dolphins are not poikilothermic; they are homeothermic (endothermic) mammals.

options.text: Leech is a fresh water form while all others are marine
options.explanation: Leeches and some species of penguins are found in freshwater environments, while dolphins and sponges are primarily associated with marine environments.

options.text: Spongilla has special collared cells called choanocytes which are not found in the remaining three
options.explanation: Spongilla is a common, widely distributed fresh water sponge belonging to phylum porifera. Canal system in Spongilla is essentially of rhagon type with choanocytes restricted to small rounded chambers. It is not found in leeches, dolphins or penguins.
Sponges lack bilateral symmetry.

questions.explanation: Spongilla is a common, widely distributed fresh water sponge belonging to phylum porifera. Canal system in Spongilla is essentially of rhagon type with choanocytes restricted to small rounded chambers. It is not found in leeches, dolphins or penguins.

questions.text: Which one of the following kinds of animals are triploblastic?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Triploblastic is a condition which describes an animal having a body composed of three embryonic germ layers : the ectoderm, mesoderm and endoderm. Most multicellular animals belonging to phylum platyhelminthes to phylum chordata are triploblastic. Ctenophores, sponges and corals are diploblastic. Flatworms belong to phylum platyhelminthes.

Sponges are diploblastic animals. Diploblastic organisms have two primary germ layers during their embryonic development: the endoderm (inner layer) and the ectoderm (outer layer). These germ layers give rise to various tissues and structures in the organism.
Cnidarians are diploblastic organisms, meaning they have two primary germ layers: the endoderm and ectoderm.
Flatworms are triploblastic.

questions.explanation: Triploblastic is a condition which describes an animal having a body composed of three embryonic germ layers : the ectoderm, mesoderm and endoderm. Most multicellular animals belonging to phylum platyhelminthes to phylum chordata are triploblastic. Ctenophores, sponges and corals are diploblastic. Flatworms belong to phylum platyhelminthes

questions.text: Which one of the following statements about certain given animals is correct?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Roundworms, or Aschelminthes, are indeed pseudocoelomates. They have a body cavity called a pseudocoelom that is partially lined with mesoderm, unlike true coelomates where the coelom is entirely surrounded by mesoderm. This pseudocoelom provides some space for organ development and circulation of fluids.

Acoelomates are animals having no body cavity or coelom. Examples are poriferans coelenterates, ctenophora, platyhelminthes and nemertinea. Molluscs are coelomates, having coelom surrounded by mesoderm.

Insects are not pseudocoelomates; they are classified as coelomates. In coelomates, the body cavity, called the coelom, is entirely surrounded by mesoderm. Pseudocoelomates have a body cavity that is only partially lined by mesoderm. Insects have a true coelom, making them coelomates.

Flatworms, or Platyhelminthes, are acoelomates, which means they lack a true body cavity (coelom). They have a solid, mesoderm-filled tissue structure and do not possess a fluid-filled coelom like coelomates.

questions.explanation: Roundworms, or Aschelminthes, are indeed pseudocoelomates. They have a body cavity called a pseudocoelom that is partially lined with mesoderm, unlike true coelomates where the coelom is entirely surrounded by mesoderm. This pseudocoelom provides some space for organ development and circulation of fluids.

questions.text: All of the following organisms are its excretory organs correctly stated except?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Humans – Kidneys, sebaceous glands and tear glands
options.explanation: Kidneys are the primary excretory organs in humans. They filter waste products and excess substances from the blood to form urine, which is then eliminated from the body. Sebaceous glands and tear glands do not serve as excretory organs; they have different functions related to skin and eye health, respectively.

options.text: Earthworm – Pharyngeal, integumentary and septal nephridia
options.explanation: Nephridia is the excretory organ of the earthworm. Earthworms have three types of nephridial structures called septal, integumentary and pharyngeal nephridia. These three nephridial structures are present on different positions in the body and also vary in structure. Septal and pharyngeal nephridia are both enteronephric i.e., nitrogen products are expelled in the gut. Integumentary nephridia is exonephric i.e., nitrogen waste products are directly discharged outside.

options.text: Cockroach – Malpighian tubules and enteric caeca
options.explanation: The excretory organs of a cockroach consist of Malpighian tubules and enteric caeca.

options.text: Frog – Kidneys, skin and buccal epithelium
options.explanation: The excretory organs of a frog are kidneys, skin, and buccal epithelium.

questions.explanation: Kidneys are the primary excretory organs in humans. They filter waste products and excess substances from the blood to form urine, which is then eliminated from the body. Sebaceous glands and tear glands do not serve as excretory organs; they have different functions related to skin and eye health, respectively.

questions.text: Crocodile and penguin are similar to whale and dogfish in which one of the following features?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Possess a solid single stranded central nervous system
options.explanation: Crocodiles and penguins possess a central nervous system, but it is not single-stranded; it includes a brain and spinal cord. In contrast, whales and dogfish also have a central nervous system but, like other vertebrates, they have a brain and spinal cord as well.

options.text: Lay eggs and guard them till they hatch
options.explanation: Crocodiles and penguins are similar in that they lay eggs and guard them, but whales and dogfish give birth to live young instead of laying eggs. So, there is a distinction between these groups in their reproductive methods.
Dogfish, also known as the spiny dogfish, is a type of cartilaginous fish and does not possess a bony skeleton like crocodiles, penguins, and whales.

options.text: Have gill slits at some stage
options.explanation: Animals belonging to Phylum Chordata are fundamentally characterised by the presence of a notochord, a dorsal hollow nerve cord and paired pharyngeal gill slits. Crocodile, penguin, whale and dogfish are all chordates. All of them have gill slits or have had them during embryonic development. Thus, paired gill slits are present in these animals at some stage of life.

questions.explanation: Animals belonging to Phylum Chordata are fundamentally characterised by the presence of a notochord, a dorsal hollow nerve cord and paired pharyngeal gill slits. Crocodile, penguin, whale and dogfish are all chordates. All of them have gill slits or have had them during embryonic development. Thus, paired gill slits are present in these animals at some stage of life.

questions.text: Which one of the following groups of animals is bilaterally symmetrical and triploblastic?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Aschelminthes is a superphylum consisting of pseudocoelomates. These are mostly aquatic, free living or parasitic. Their body is slender, bilaterally symmetrical and triploblastic.

Ctenophores, also called comb jellies, are radially or biradially symmetrical animals, meaning their body can be divided into similar parts through several planes, not just one. They are also diploblastic, having only two germ layers the ectoderm and endoderm with no mesoderm, so they cannot be called triploblastic.
Sponges are asymmetrical and diploblastic organisms. They lack true tissue layers, organs, and symmetry typical of more complex animals.
Coelenterates, also known as cnidarians, are diploblastic organisms and exhibit radial symmetry.

questions.explanation: Aschelminthes is a superphylum consisting of pseudocoelomates. These are mostly aquatic, free living or parasitic. Their body is slender, bilaterally symmetrical and triploblastic.

questions.text: If a live earthworm is pricked with a needle on its outer surface without damaging its gut, the fluid that comes out is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Coelom or body cavity of earthworm is filled with coelomic fluid. It lies between body wall and alimentary canal. So if a live earthworm is pricked with a needle on its outer surface without damaging the gut then only coelomic fluid will come out.
No, earthworms do not have haemolymph. They have a circulatory system with a fluid called coelomic fluid that serves functions similar to hemolymph in some other invertebrates.
Coelom or body cavity of earthworm is filled with coelomic fluid, not slimy mucus.
Coelom or body cavity of earthworm is filled with coelomic fluid, not excretory fluid.

questions.explanation: Coelom or body cavity of earthworm is filled with coelomic fluid. It lies between body wall and alimentary canal. So if a live earthworm is pricked with a needle on its outer surface without damaging the gut then only coelomic fluid will come out.

questions.text: Which one of the following pairs of animals comprise 'jawless fishes’?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mackerels and rohu are not jawless fishes. Both are jawed fishes, which means they have well-developed jaws with teeth.

Agnatha is subphylum or superclass of marine and fresh water vertebrates that lack jaws. They are fishlike animals with cartilaginous skeletons and well developed sucking mouthparts with horny teeth. The only living agnathans are lampreys and hagfishes (Class Cyclostomata), which are parasites or scavengers.
Guppies are not jawless fishes. They are part of the Osteichthyes group, which includes jawed bony fishes.
Eels are not jawless fishes. They are part of the Osteichthyes group, which includes jawed bony fishes. Eels have well-developed jaws with teeth.

questions.explanation: Agnatha is subphylum or superclass of marine and fresh water vertebrates that lack jaws. They are fishlike animals with cartilaginous skeletons and well developed sucking mouthparts with horny teeth. The only living agnathans are lampreys and hagfishes (Class Cyclostomata), which are parasites or scavengers.

questions.text: Ascaris is characterized by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Presence of true coelom but absence of metamerism
options.explanation: False, because Ascaris does not have a true coelom (it has a pseudocoelom).

options.text: Presence of true coelom and metamerism (metamerisation)
options.explanation: False, because Ascaris has neither true coelom nor segmentation.These features are found in higher animals like Annelida (e.g., earthworm).

options.text: Absence of true coelom but presence of metamerism
options.explanation: False, because although it lacks a true coelom, it also lacks metamerism.

options.text: Presence of neither true coelom nor metamerism
options.explanation: Ascaris belong to the Phylum Nematoda of Superphylum Aschelminthes.They have a cylindrical body without showing any metamerism, a pseudocoelom (false coelom) and a complete digestive tract lined by endodermal epithelium. The cuticle covering the body surface bears minute transverse striations giving a pseudosegmented appearance to the worm.

questions.explanation: Ascaris belong to the Phylum Nematoda of Superphylum Aschelminthes.They have a cylindrical body without showing any metamerism, a pseudocoelom (false coelom) and a complete digestive tract lined by endodermal epithelium. The cuticle covering the body surface bears minute transverse striations giving a pseudosegmented appearance to the worm.

questions.text: Which one of the following pairs of items correctly belongs to the category of organs mentioned against it?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Nephridia of earthworm and Malpighian tubules of cockroach - excretory organs
options.explanation: Nephridia are excretory organs in earthworms, responsible for removing waste and excess water from the body. Malpighian tubules are excretory organs in cockroaches and some other insects, responsible for removing waste and maintaining the water balance in their bodies.

options.text: Wings of honey bee and wings of crow - homologous organs
options.explanation: Wings of honey bee and wings of crow are analogous organs as they are functionally similar, not structurally.

options.text: Thorn of Bougainvillea and tendrils of Cucurbita - analogous organs
options.explanation: Thorns and tendrils serve different functions and are not considered analogous organs in biological terms.

options.text: Nictitating membrane and blind spot in human eye - vestigial organs
options.explanation: Nictitating membrane is a vestigial structure (a reduced and non-functional organ that had a function in ancestors), and the blind spot is a natural feature in the human eye but not considered a vestigial organ.

questions.explanation: Nephridia are excretory organs in earthworms, responsible for removing waste and excess water from the body. Malpighian tubules are excretory organs in cockroaches and some other insects, responsible for removing waste and maintaining the water balance in their bodies.

questions.text: Which one of the following phyla is correctly matched with its two general characteristics?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Echinodermata - pentamerous radial symmetry and mostly internal fertilization
options.explanation: Echinodermata exhibit pentamerous radial symmetry, which means their bodies are organized in a five-part radial pattern. However, most echinoderms undergo external fertilization, not internal fertilization.

options.text: Mollusca - normally oviparous and development through a trochophore or veliger larva
options.explanation: Some mollusks have direct development, where they do not have free-swimming larvae and instead develop directly into miniature versions of the adult.

options.text: Arthropoda - body divided into head, thorax and abdomen and respiration by tracheae
options.explanation: Arthropods are the largest phylum of Kingdom Animalia that characteristically possesses an outer body layer – the cuticle. Their body is composed of segments usually forming distinct specialized body regions, i.e., head, thorax and abdomen. In them the trachea or windpipe or book lungs are the respiratory organs found in terrestrial forms, which help in respiration.

options.text: Chordata - notochord at some stage and separate anal and urinary openings to the outside
options.explanation: Chordata is characterized by the presence of a notochord at some stage in their development. However, not all chordates have separate anal and urinary openings to the outside.

questions.explanation: Arthropods are the largest phylum of Kingdom Animalia that characteristically possesses an outer body layer – the cuticle. Their body is composed of segments usually forming distinct specialized body regions, i.e., head, thorax and abdomen. In them the trachea or windpipe or book lungs are the respiratory organs found in terrestrial forms, which help in respiration.

questions.text: Which one of the following is not a characteristic of Phylum Annelida?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phylum Annelida comprises invertebrates, which are segmented worms having cylindrical soft bodies showing metameric segmentation. These are triploblastic animals showing bilateral symmetry. A true coelom is present which is filled with coelomic fluid containing cells. Annelids are perhaps the first animals to have a true schizocoelic coelom.

Yes, annelids, which are segmented worms, have a ventral nerve cord. This nerve cord runs along the underside (ventral) of their body and is a key part of their nervous system, helping them coordinate movement and sensory functions.
Annelids were the first one to introduce closed circulatory system.
Yes, annelids are segmented organisms, meaning their bodies are divided into distinct segments or repeated units.

questions.explanation: Phylum Annelida includes segmented worms such as earthworms, leeches, and polychaetas. These animals have a true coelom (a body cavity completely lined with mesoderm), not a pseudocoelom. A pseudocoelom is a body cavity that is not fully lined with mesoderm and is found in organisms like roundworms (Phylum Nematoda).

questions.text: Which of the following pairs are correctly matched? (i) Crocodile & 4 chambered heart (ii) Sea urchin & Parapodia (iii) Obelia & Metagenesis (iv) Lemur & Thecodont
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: (ii) is wrong and (i) is also correct. Crocodiles are four chambered heart.
(iii) is also correct. Obelia shows metagenesis i.e., alternation of generations which may be defined as a phenomenon in which diploid asexual phase alternates with haploid sexual phase.
(ii) is wrong and (iv) is also correct. Lemur has thecodont teeth i.e., the teeth are embedded in the sockets of the jaw bone.

Reptiles have two auricles and a partly divided ventricle except crocodiles which have a four chambered heart. Obelia shows metagenesis i.e., alternation of generations which may be defined as a phenomenon in which diploid asexual phase alternates with haploid sexual phase. Lemur has thecodont teeth i.e., the teeth are embedded in the sockets of the jaw bone.

questions.explanation: Reptiles have two auricles and a partly divided ventricle except crocodiles which have a four chambered heart. Obelia shows metagenesis i.e., alternation of generations which may be defined as a phenomenon in which diploid asexual phase alternates with haploid sexual phase. Lemur has thecodont teeth i.e., the teeth are embedded in the sockets of the jaw bone.

questions.text: What is common between parrot, platypus and kangaroo?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Parrots and duckbills (platypuses) both have toothless jaws. Parrots have beaks that lack teeth, and platypuses have a specialized jaw structure with horny plates for grinding food. Kangaroos, on the other hand, have complex molars for grinding plant material, and they are not considered toothless.
No, parrots, duckbills (platypuses), and kangaroos do not have functional postanal tails. While they are all vertebrates, they do not possess tails like those found in some other.
Kangaroois is viviparous.

Homoiothermy is the maintenance by an animal of its internal body temperature at a relatively constant value by using metabolic processes to counteract fluctuations in the temperature of the environment. Homoiothermy occurs in birds and mammals, which are described as endotherms. The heat produced by their tissue metabolism and the heat lost to the environment are balanced by various means to keep body temperature constant: 36-38ºC in mammals and 38-40ºC in birds. The hypothalamus in the brain monitors blood temperature and controls thermoregulation by both nervous and hormonal means. Thus, parrots (birds), platypuses and kangaroos (mammals) are homoiothermic animals.

questions.explanation: Homoiothermy is the maintenance by an animal of its internal body temperature at a relatively constant value by using metabolic processes to counteract fluctuations in the temperature of the environment. Homoiothermy occurs in birds and mammals, which are described as endotherms. The heat produced by their tissue metabolism and the heat lost to the environment are balanced by various means to keep body temperature constant: 36-38ºC in mammals and 38-40ºC in birds. The hypothalamus in the brain monitors blood temperature and controls thermoregulation by both nervous and hormonal means. Thus, parrots (birds), platypuses and kangaroos (mammals) are homoiothermic animals.

questions.text: What is true about Nereis, scorpion, cockroach and silver fish?

questions.text: Biradial symmetry and lack of cnidoblasts are the characteristics of:

Start fish and sea anemone both are cnidarians. Thus, possess both cnidoblasts and lack biradial symmetry. Both of them are marine some species are motile. Starfish has medusae as dominant form while Sea anemone exhibits polymorphism. They have double layer of organisation.

They lack the specialized stinging cells (nematocysts) found in coelenterates, but one species (Haeckelia rubra) incorporates those of its jellyfish prey for its own defense.
Aurelia is a jellyfish. A cnidarian which has medusae as dominant form.Paramecium is an acoelomate belonging to the phylum platyhelminthes.

questions.text: Which one of the following is not a living fossil?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is also called as velvet worms and resemble caterpillars. These are primitive arthropods. These have joint pairs of legs and trachea-like arthropods. King crab (Arthropoda), sphenodon (Reptilia) and peripatus (an invertebrate) are examples of living fossils.
King crab (Arthropoda), sphenodon (Reptilia) and peripatus (an invertebrate) are examples of living fossils.
King crab (Arthropoda), sphenodon (Reptilia) and peripatus (an invertebrate) are examples of living fossils.

The earliest known bird fossil is that of archaeopteryx, two species of which have been found from rocks of Jurassic period of earth’s history. The fossil shows that archaeopteryx, was about the size of a crow with skull similar to that of present day birds. It had bony teeth in the jaw socket unlike modem birds which do not have teeth. Jaws extended into a beak and there was a long tail. Each wing had three claws. With the exception of feathers these birds showed resemblance to the dinosaurs (giant reptiles of the past). Many fossils of birds from later eras of earth history have also been found that had teeth. The above evidence suggests that birds evolved from reptilian ancestors. The archaeopteryx and others had characteristic of both reptiles and birds and therefore form a connecting link between the two distinct groups.

questions.explanation: The earliest known bird fossil is that of Archaeopteryx, two species of which have been found in rocks from the Jurassic period of Earth's history. The fossil shows that Archaeopteryx was about the size of a crow with a skull similar to that of present-day birds. It had bony teeth in the jaw socket, unlike modern birds, which do not have teeth. The jaws extended into a beak and there was a long tail. Each wing had three claws. With the exception of feathers, these birds showed a resemblance to the dinosaurs (giant reptiles of the past). Many fossils of birds from later eras of Earth's history have also been found that had teeth. The above evidence suggests that birds evolved from reptilian ancestors. Archaeopteryx and others had characteristics of both reptiles and birds and therefore form a connecting link between the two distinct groups

questions.text: Annual migration does not occur in the case of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Arctic tern- They are very small birds that have the longest yearly annual migration of any animal on earth. Every year these birds migrate from the arctic to the Antarctic Circle by making a round trip journey of about eighteen thousand miles. So this makes the Arctic tern the longest migrating animal on the earth.

Salmon- Ocean migration occurs, depending on the kind of species salmon will spend nearly years of feeding on the high seas until their spawning migration begins. During this time salmon stops eating and they rely on their stored energy to power up their migration journey.

Siberian Crane- The eastern population will migrate during the winter season to China while the western population will migrate to Iran and formerly, to India and Bharatpur. Among cranes, these types make the longest distance of migration.
Salamander- These animals do not migrate but when the snow starts to melt and when it rains they tend to go to a warmer place so they hibernate in the underground near to the pond.

questions.explanation: Salamander- These animals do not migrate but when the snow starts to melt and when it rains they tend to go to a warmer place so they hibernate in the underground near to the pond.

questions.text: Metameric segmentation is the characteristic of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Molluscs have segmentatio but primitively and not in later stage whereas chordates also exhibit metamaerism in embryonic stages.
Annelida and Arthropoda are the only phylum which shows the metamerism segmentation feature.
Annelida and Arthropoda are the only phylum which shows the metamerism segmentation feature.

The term metamerism refers to a linear repetition of parts in an animal body. It occurs in three highly organized phyla : Annelida, Arthropoda and Chordata. Each segment is called a metamere, or somite. Segmentation often affects both external and internal structures. Such a condition is called metameric segmentation. In chordates, the segmentation is apparent only in the embryonic stage. In the adult chordates, segmentation is visible in the internal structures, such as vertebrae, ribs, nerves and blood vessels. Other animals have unsegmented bodies.

questions.explanation: The term metamerism refers to a linear repetition of parts in an animal body. It occurs in three highly organized phyla : Annelida, Arthropoda and Chordata. Each segment is called a metamere, or somite. Segmentation often affects both external and internal structures. Such a condition is called metameric segmentation. In chordates, the segmentation is apparent only in the embryonic stage. In the adult chordates, segmentation is visible in the internal structures, such as vertebrae, ribs, nerves and blood vessels. Other animals have unsegmented bodies.

questions.text: Which one of the following is a matching set of a phylum and its three examples?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Spongilla and Euplectella belong to porifera but Pennatula (the sea pen or sea feather) belongs to coelenterata.
Physalia and Aurelia belong to cnidaria but Bonellia belongs to Phylum Annelida.
Planaria and Schistosoma belong to platyhelminthes but Enterobius (Pinworm) belongs to schelminthes.

Mollusca includes animals that have soft bodies, usually furnished with a shell. The body is often divided into a head with eyes or tentacles, a muscular foot, and a visceral mass housing the organs. Loligo (squid or sea arrow), Teredo (shipworm), and Octopus are some examples.

questions.explanation: Mollusca includes animals that have soft bodies, usually furnished with a shell. The body is often divided into a head with eyes or tentacles, a muscular foot, and a visceral mass housing the organs. Loligo (squid or sea arrow), Teredo (shipworm), and Octopus are some examples. In option (a), Spongilla and Euplectella belong to Porifera, but Pennatula (the sea pen or sea feather) belongs to Coelenterata. In option (b), Physalia and Aurelia belong to Cnidaria, but Bonellia belongs to the phylum Annelida. In option (c), Planaria and Schistosoma belong to Platyhelminthes, but Enterobius (pinworm) belongs to aschaleminthes.

questions.text: In contrast to annelids the platyhelminthes show :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Platyhelminthes do not have body cavity so they are acoelomates. In annelids, the body cavity is true and schizocoelous. Both annelids and platyhelminthes have bilateral symmetry.
Both possess bilateral symmetry.
Both lack radial symmetry.
Pseudocoel is not present in both.

questions.explanation: Platyhelminthes (flatworms) don’t have a body cavity, so they are called acoelomates (animals without a body cavity). In annelids (segmented worms), the body cavity is a true coelom, formed by splitting of the mesoderm (this type is called schizocoelous). Both annelids and platyhelminthes have bilateral symmetry, which means their body can be divided into two equal halves (left and right) along a single plane.

questions.text: From the following statements select the wrong one:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Prawn has one pairs of antennae
options.explanation: Prawns have two pairs of antennae. One pair is called antennae and the other is called antennules.Antennae: These are located on each side of the head, just below the antennules. They help the prawn in three ways:Sensory – they detect touch, chemicals, and movements in the water.Excretory – they are connected to special glands that help remove waste from the body.Balancing – they help the prawn maintain balance while moving in water.Antennules: These are attached on each side, just below the eye stalks. Their main role is tactile function, meaning they sense touch and vibrations, helping the prawn understand its surroundings.

options.text: Nematocysts are characteristics of the phylum cnidaria
options.explanation: Nematocysts are present in cnidoblasts that act as organs of offence and defence.

options.text: Millepedes have two pairs of appendages in each segment of the body
options.explanation: Millipedes belong to class myriapoda. They are called thousand leggers because of possession of numerous walking legs. Their body is made up of a small head and 40 trunk segments, each with two pairs of jointed legs.

options.text: Animals belonging to phylum porifera are marine and fresh water
options.explanation: Animals belonging to phylum porifera are mostly marine while a few are freshwater.

questions.explanation: Prawns have two pairs of antennae, one on either side, just below the antennules. They are sensory, excretory, and balancing in function. Antennules are attached on either side, below the bases of the eye stalks. They are tactile in function. Nematocysts are present in cnidoblasts and act as organs of offence and defence. Millipedes belong to the class Myriapoda. They are called thousand-leggers because they possess numerous walking legs. Their body is made up of a small head and 40 trunk segments, each with two pairs of jointed legs. Animals belonging to the phylum Porifera are mostly marine, while a few are freshwater.

questions.text: Which one of the following characters is not typical of the class mammalia?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Thecodont dentition is a morphological arrangement in which the base of the tooth is completely enclosed in a deep socket of bone, as seen in crocodilians, dinosaurs and mammals, and opposed to acrodont and pleurodont dentition seen in squamate reptiles.
Alveolar lungs are present in all mammals.

Cranial nerves are the nerves that arise directly from the brain (not from the spinal cord). They control important functions such as smell, sight, hearing, balance, movement of facial muscles, and sensations from the head.Mammals: They have 12 pairs of cranial nerves. These nerves perform advanced functions like controlling complex senses (smell, vision, hearing), movements of the face, tongue, and even regulating organs in the chest and abdomen.Fish and Amphibians: They have only 10 pairs of cranial nerves. Their nervous system is simpler compared to mammals, so they lack the two additional pairs.Reptiles and Birds: Like mammals, they also have 12 pairs of cranial nerves, which allow better control of their senses and body functions compared to amphibians and fish.ves.
Seven verterbra are present in mammals.
