questions.text: In Arthropoda, head and thorax are often used to form cephalothorax, but in which one of the following classes is the body divided into the head, thorax and abdomen?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Insects form the largest group, not only within the phylum Arthropoda but also in the entire animal kingdom. This means they are the most numerous and diverse animals on Earth. There are millions of insect species, and they live in almost every type of environment—land, water, air, soil, forests, deserts, and even inside other animals and plants.Many insects also show social behaviour (living and working together in organized groups). For example, ants, bees, and termites live in colonies where different members have specific roles (like workers, soldiers, and queens).The insect body is divided into three distinct regions:Head – contains the eyes, antennae (for sensing), and mouthparts.Thorax – has three pairs of legs and usually one or two pairs of wings, making insects the only invertebrates that can fly.Abdomen – contains most of the internal organs, including those for digestion, excretion, and reproduction.
The body is divided into large number of segments each having a pair of legs. A pair of antennae and a pair of eyes are present on head e.g. centipedes and millipedes.

These arthropods are aquatic and have gills for respiration. On the dorsal side of the cephalothorax the exoskeleton is in the fofm of carapace. In the exoskeleton deposition of salts in addition to chitin makes it more firm. The appendages are modiied for capturing food, walking, swimming, respiration and reproduction.

Body has the anterior segments that are fused to form a combined cephalothorax, with a pair of appendages called chelicerae with claws, two pairs as pedipalps and four pairs of legs. There are no antennae and no true jaws. Abdomen may be segmented or unsegmented with or without appendages.

questions.explanation: Class insecta is the largest group not only of Arthropoda but of all the animal kingdom and has great variety. Insects are found everywhere, many show social behaviour. The body in, insects has three distinct regions head, thorax and abdomen.

questions.text: The animals with bilateral symmetry in young stage and radial pentamerous symmetry in the adult stage belong to the phylum:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Annelids display bilateral symmetry and are worm-like in overall morphology. They have a segmented body plan where the internal and external morphological features are repeated in each body segment.
Mollusks have bilateral symmetry. – The two halves of the body mirror each other.
Most of the cnidarians exhibit radial symmetry, wherein the symmetry around a center point, such that any line drawn through the center of the entity divides the body into mirror images.

Echinoderms (like starfish, sea urchins, brittle stars, and sea cucumbers) are special because they are different from most other animals.All modern echinoderms show pentaradial symmetry, meaning their body parts are arranged in a pattern based on five arms or sections (like a starfish with five arms).However, when we look at the fossil record, we find that ancient echinoderms were more diverse in body forms. Some were bilateral (divided into two equal halves like humans), some were asymmetrical (irregular in shape), and others showed different kinds of radial symmetry (not just five-fold)

Some were bilateral (divided into two equal halves like humans), some were asymmetrical (irregular in shape), and others showed different kinds of radial symmetry (not just five-fold)

questions.text: Presence of gills in the tadpole of frog indicates that:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Fish were amphibians in the past
options.explanation: Fish are the ancestors of amphibians, not the other way around.
Fish are the ancesestors of amphibians.

options.text: Frogs will have gills in future
options.explanation: Future of evolution can not be predicted but frogs have gills in embryonic stage just as their ancestors did. Amphibians evolved from fish.

Amphibians are on the borderline between aquatic and true terrestrial animals. Fossil evidence from the Devonian period of Earth’s history suggests that a large population of fish belonging to the group lobe-fins (Dipnoi) lived in shallow freshwater. Some of these crawled from one pool to another and therefore spent some time on land. This gave rise to the group that we recognize as amphibians, which are the first vertebrates to come onto land. This suggests that frogs, i.e., amphibians, are from gilled ancestors

questions.explanation: Amphibians are on the borderline between aquatic and true terrestrial animals. Fossil evidence from the Devonian period of Earth’s history suggests that a large population of fish belonging to the group lobe-fins (Dipnoi) lived in shallow freshwater. Some of these crawled from one pool to another and therefore spent some time on land. This gave rise to the group that we recognize as amphibians, which are the first vertebrates to come onto land. This suggests that frogs, i.e., amphibians, are from gilled ancestors

questions.text: Sycon belongs to a group of animals which are best described as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as Sycon is a multicellular organism, belonging to the Phylum Porifera, which is characterized by a lack of true tissues.
This is the correct answer, as Sycon is a multicellular organism that lacks true tissues, displaying a cellular level of organization.
This option is incorrect because Sycon does not possess a gastrovascular system; instead, it has a simple body structure without organized tissues.

options.text: Multicellular having tissue organization but no body cavity.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect, as Sycon does not have any tissue organization; it operates at a cellular level without true tissues.

questions.explanation: Sycon, belonging to the Phylum Porifera, are multicellular organisms with cellular level of body organisation. The constituent cells perform their functions more or less independently. No distinct tissue or organs are present in it.

questions.text: During the lifecycle Fasciola hepatica(liver fluke) infects its intermediate host and primary host at the following larval stages respectively:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: because the intermediate host is not infected by redia (redia forms inside the snail itself).And the primary host is not infected by miracidium; it is infected by metacercaria.
because the snail (intermediate host) is never infected by cercaria; it is infected by miracidium.Also, the primary host is not infected by redia, but by metacercaria.
because the intermediate host is not infected by metacercaria; it is infected by miracidium.And the primary host is not infected by cercaria; it is infected by metacercaria.

The miracidium is the first larval stage of Fasciola hepatica. It swims in water and infects the snail (the intermediate host).Inside the snail, the miracidium changes into other larval forms (sporocyst → redia → cercaria).The cercaria larva comes out of the snail into the water.The cercaria then attaches to water plants and becomes a metacercaria (encysted stage).The metacercaria is the stage that infects the primary host (sheep, cow, or human) when the plant is eaten.Once inside the body of the primary host, the metacercaria develops into the adult liver fluke

questions.explanation: The life cycle of F. hepatica is completed in two hosts. The primary host, in which the adult fluke lives, is a sheep, while the intermediate host, in which numerous larval stages are passed, is a snail (Lymnaea, Planorbis, etc.). This type of life cycle involving two different kinds of hosts is as termed digenetic. Miracidium larva is the larval stage involved in the life cycle. When suitable conditions become available, the encapsulated embryo differentiates into a miracidium larva in 415 days. It hatches out and swims in water. Metacercaria develops into adult fluke only inside its definitive host i.e sheep. The latter gets an infection by grazing on leaves and grass blades to which the cysts are attached. Metacercaria survives action of its host’s gastric juices as its cysts are insoluble in them. The cyst wall finally dissolves in the proximal part of the intestine and liberates the larva..

questions.text: Ommatidia serve the purpose of photoreception in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cockroaches have compound eyes, which means each eye is made up of many small units called ommatidia.Each ommatidium acts like a tiny eye, with its own lens and light-sensitive cells.All the ommatidia together form a mosaic image of what the cockroach sees.This type of vision is very good for detecting light changes and movements, even if the image is not very sharp or detailed.Because of this, cockroaches can quickly sense danger or motion around them, which is why they escape so fast when disturbed.
Frogs have well-developed eyes adapted for vision both in and out of water. Their eyes have different adaptations than ommatidia.
Humans have complex eyes with a single lens, unlike the compound eyes of insects. Our eyes have a retina with photoreceptor cells called rods and cones for vision.
Sunflowers do not have eyes or any form of photoreception organs like animals. They exhibit heliotropism, turning their faces toward the sun, but this is a growth response, not vision.

questions.explanation: In cockroaches, the compound eyes are a pair of large, black, kidney shaped organs situated dorso-laterally on the head, one on either side. Their surface is marked by a large number of hexagonal areas, the facets. Each facet represents a visual unit named ommatidium. The eyes are the organs of sight (photoreception).In summary, ommatidia are specifically associated with the compound eyes of insects, such as cockroaches, for visual perception.

questions.text: Which one of the following is a matching pair of an animal and a certain phenomenon it exhibits?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Pheretima – Sexual dimorphism: Pheretima, commonly known as earthworms, typically exhibits sexual dimorphism, where male and female individuals have distinct reproductive structures.
Musca – Complete metamorphosis: Musca, which refers to flies like houseflies, undergoes complete metamorphosis, involving four stages: egg, larva (maggot), pupa, and adult.
Chameleon – Mimicry: Chameleons are known for their ability to change color for communication, temperature regulation, and camouflage. However, mimicry is not a characteristic behavior of chameleons.
Taenia – Polymorphism: Taenia, referring to tapeworms, displays polymorphism where different forms of individuals exist within the same species, particularly in terms of reproductive structures.

questions.explanation: In Musca (housefly), development is indirect with complete metamorphosis (holometaboly), which includes four stages: egg, larva, pupa, and adult. In complete metamorphosis, the larva moults several times after hatching to grow into its full size. It then transforms into a pupa within a secreted case called the puparium. Inside the puparium, the pupa differentiates into a young adult, which later breaks open the puparium and emerges outside. The young adult then grows into the mature form

questions.text: Given below are four matchings of an animal and its kind of respiratory organ:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A and B are matched correctly but sea squirt is also matched correctly. So, we need to select the option with all three of them.

Silver fish is an insect in which respiration occurs by tracheae. These communicate with the exterior by paired apertures, called spiracles. The respiratory system of the scorpion consists of 4 pairs of book lungs that communicate with the outer air through stigma. In sea squirts, respiration occurs through pharyngeal slits.
Dolphins respire by lungs. They do not use skin as a respiratory organ unlike some amphibians.
Dolphins use lungs as respiratory organs. Sea squirt on the hand is matched correctly.

questions.explanation: Silverfish is an insect in which respiration occurs through tracheae. These communicate with the exterior by paired apertures called spiracles. The respiratory system of the scorpion consists of four pairs of book lungs that communicate with the outer air through stigmata. In sea squirts, respiration occurs through pharyngeal slits, while in dolphins, respiration occurs through lungs. Silverfish and scorpions belong to the phylum Arthropoda, while sea squirts and dolphins belong to the phylum Chordata

questions.text: In which of the following animals is the brain absent but nerve cells are present?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sponges are the only multicellular animals without a nervous system. They do not have any nerve cells or sensory cells.
The nervous system of an earthworm comprises of a ventral nerve cord and peripheral nerves.
The cockroach's nervous system is composed of a series of fused, segmentally organised ganglia connected by paired longitudinal connectives on the ventral side.

Hydra belongs to the phylum Coelenterata and has nerve cells but no brain. Its nervous system consists of nerve cells and their processes, and sensory cells are also present. Sponges do not have nerve cells because they lack a nervous system. The earthworm (Annelida) has a nervous system consisting of a circumenteric nerve ring and a solid, double, mid-ventral nerve cord with ganglia. The cockroach (Arthropoda) has a nervous system similar to that of the earthworm.

questions.explanation: Hydra belongs to the phylum Coelenterata and has nerve cells but no brain. Its nervous system consists of nerve cells and their processes, and sensory cells are also present. The cockroach (Arthropoda) has a nervous system similar to that of the earthworm.

questions.text: In which of the following is a notochord present in the embryonic stage?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chordates are animals that have a notochord, a skeletal rod present at some stage in their life cycle. In lower vertebrates, the notochord persists throughout life, while in higher vertebrates it is replaced by the vertebral column in adults. Non-chordates never develop a notochord, not even in the embryonic stage.
All chordates have notochord in embryonic stage of their lives.
Not all vertebrates have notochord in embryonic stage. Some vertebrates have larval forms before they develop into complete adults.
Nonchordates never develop a notochord, not even in the embryonic stage.

questions.explanation: Chordates are animals that have a notochord, a skeletal rod present at some stage in their life cycle. In lower vertebrates, the notochord persists throughout life, while in higher vertebrates it is replaced by the vertebral column in adults. Non-chordates never develop a notochord, not even in the embryonic stage.

questions.text: In which of the following animals is the haemocyanin pigment found?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Annelida does not have heamocyanin as pigment.Most annelids use haemoglobin (iron-containing, red pigment) dissolved in plasma or inside cells.That’s why their blood is red, not blue.
They generally lack any respiratory pigment like haemoglobin or haemocyanin.Instead, gas exchange occurs by diffusion through body surfaces, tube feet, and papulae

Insects don’t need respiratory pigments at all.They have a tracheal system, where air enters directly into tissues through spiracles and tracheae.Since oxygen is delivered directly to cells, their blood (haemolymph) is colorless and doesn’t carry oxygen.

In molluscs, blood often contains a copper-containing blue respiratory pigment called haemocyanin. In insects, the blood, called haemolymph, is colourless. Blood is also colourless in echinoderms, as it has no respiratory pigment. In annelids, the blood is red, with haemoglobin dissolved in the plasma.

questions.explanation: In molluscs, blood often has a copper containing, blue, respiratory pigment called haemocyanin. In insects, the blood called haemolymph is colourless. Blood is also colourless in echinodermates as it has no respiratory pigment. In annelids, the blood is red with haemoglobin dissolved in plasma.

questions.text: In which of the following animals is a post anal tail found?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Earthworms do not contain post anal tail.
Post anal tail is present in vertebrates only.
Scorpion is an arthropod and thus does not possess post anal tail. Post anal tail is an extension of vertebral column.

The post-anal tail is a posterior elongation of the body that extends beyond the anus. It contains skeletal elements and muscles, providing locomotion in aquatic species. In some terrestrial vertebrates, the tail also aids in balance, courtship, and signaling danger.

questions.explanation: Snakes are limbless reptiles with an elongated cylindrical body that is covered with overlapping scales differentiated into shields and plates. They also have a long, post anal tail.The post-anal tail is a posterior elongation of the body that extends beyond the anus. It contains skeletal elements and muscles, providing locomotion in aquatic species. In some terrestrial vertebrates, the tail also aids in balance, courtship, and signaling danger.

questions.text: In Hydra waste material of food digestion and nitrogenous waste material are removed respectively from:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Wrong because nitrogenous waste does not pass through the mouth — it diffuses out through the body wall.
Wrong because undigested food is not expelled through the body wall; it goes out of the mouth.

In Hydra, indigestible residues are egested through the mouth as there is no anus. Egestion occurs by a sudden squirt due to muscular contraction of body, so that the debris is thrown at a distance. Hydra has neither blood or blood vessels, nor organs of excretion. Due to thinness of the body wall and circulation of water in gastrovascular cavity, most cells of the body remain freely exposed to the surrounding water. Therefore, excretion of waste nitrogenous matter (chiefly ammonia) occurs directly by diffusion through cell membranes in the outside world.
Tentacles are for capturing prey, not for excretion.So both parts of this option are incorrect.

questions.explanation: In Hydra, indigestible residues are egested through the mouth as there is no anus. Egestion occurs by a sudden squirt due to muscular contraction of body, so that the debris is thrown at a distance. Hydra has neither blood or blood vessels, nor organs of excretion. Due to thinness of the body wall and circulation of water in gastrovascular cavity, most cells of the body remain freely exposed to the surrounding water. Therefore, excretion of waste nitrogenous matter (chiefly ammonia) occurs directly by diffusion through cell membranes in the outside world.

questions.text: Cleavage in mammals is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mammals do not exhibit this cleavage.

Cleavage in mammals is holoblastic and unequal. Mammals have microlecithal eggs, so they undergo holoblastic cleavage, in which the segmentation lines pass through the entire egg, dividing it completely. Since the eggs are microlecithal, one would expect the first cleavage to produce two equal blastomeres. However, this is not the case. The two blastomeres produced are unequal, and they divide further to form four unequal blastomeres. This process continues until a ball of cells, called the morula, is formed. Superficial cleavage occurs in insects, while discoidal cleavage occurs in birds.
Superficial cleavage is not present in mammals.
Discoidal cleavage is not present in mammals.

questions.explanation: Cleavage in mammals is holoblastic unequal. Mammals have microlecithal eggs so they have holoblastic cleavage in which the segmentation lines pass through the entire egg, dividing it completely. As the eggs are microlecithal so one would expect that first cleavage will produce two equal blastomeres. But, this is not the case. The two blastomeres produced are unequal which divide further to form 4 unequal blastomeres and this process continues to form a ball of cells called morula. Superficial cleavage occurs in insects and discoidal cleavage occurs in birds.

questions.text: Which of the following animals have scattered cells with cell tissue grade organisation?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sponges do not have any grade of organisation. They lack organisation of any kind.
Hydra has reached the tissue grade of organization, but it lacks organs. It consists of a tube about 5 mm long formed by two epithelial layers (endoderm and ectoderm).
Liver fluke blelongs to phylum platyhelminthes and eshibits organ level of organisation.
Ascaris belongs to phylum nematoda and exhibits organ level of organisation.

questions.explanation: Hydra shows a tissue level of organization. This means its body is made up of many cells that are grouped into two main layers (tissues), and these tissues have specialized functions.Sponges are simpler and show only a cellular level of organization. Their cells work independently, without forming true tissues.Liver fluke and Ascaris are more advanced and show an organ-system level of organization. Their cells form tissues, tissues form organs, and the organs work together in systems (like digestive or reproductive systems) to keep the body functioning.

questions.text: Similarity in Ascaris lumbricoides and Anopheles stephensi is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Both Ascaris lumbricoides and Anopheles stephensi have different males and females, which can be distinguished morphologically. Thus, they show sexual dimorphism.
Metamerism is not the common characteristic of Ascaris and Anopheles.
Anerobic respiration is not common between Anopheles and Ascaris.
Endoparasites are parasites that live in the tissues and organs of their hosts, such as tapeworms, flukes, and protozoans of vertebrates.

questions.explanation: Sexual dimorphism means the males and females of the same species look different from each other in certain physical traits.In Ascaris (a roundworm):The sexes are separate (male and female are different individuals).Males are smaller than females.Males have a recurved tail, which helps in mating.They also have pre-anal and post-anal papillae (small sensory projections).The male has a cloaca (common opening for digestive and reproductive tracts).Males also possess a pair of spicules or penial setae, which are structures that help transfer sperm during copulation.In Anopheles (mosquito):The maxillary palps (sensory appendages near the mouth) are club-shaped in males.In females, they are straight and not club-shaped.This difference helps in distinguishing male and female mosquitoes

questions.text: What is true for mammalia?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The platypus is a special kind of mammal called a monotreme. Unlike most mammals, which are viviparous (give birth to live young), the platypus is oviparous—it lays eggs.After the eggs hatch, the mother feeds the babies with milk, just like other mammals. However, the platypus does not have nipples; instead, milk is secreted from mammary glands and absorbed through the skin or fur, and the young lap it up.

Anatomically, bats are similar to other mammals and actually share a lot of the same skeletal structure as humans, including having five digits. Bats have fur, birds have feathers. Bats have teeth, birds have beaks.
Elephants are infact viviparous and they give birth to their young ones.
Diaphragm is not absent in mammals.

questions.explanation: The platypus is a special kind of mammal called a monotreme. However, the platypus does not have nipples; instead, milk is secreted from mammary glands and absorbed through the skin or fur, and the young lap it up.

questions.text: Aquatic reptiles are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Incomplete not all reptiles are ureotelic; only aquatic reptiles are.Terrestrial reptiles are uricotelic.→ So this option is too general.

Aquatic reptiles (like turtles, crocodiles):Their waste is mainly urea.Because they live in water, they can afford to lose water while excreting.Animals that excrete waste mainly as urea are called ureotelic.Land reptiles (like lizards, snakes):Their waste is mainly uric acid.Uric acid is almost solid and helps conserve water.Animals that excrete waste mainly as uric acid are called uricotelic.
Ammonia needs a lot of water for dilution and is very toxic.Even aquatic reptiles do not excrete ammonia they excrete urea instead.
Over land, Reptiles excrete nitrogenous wastes as uric acid in form of a pellet or paste with a minimum loss of water and are called uricotelic animals.

questions.text: Temperature changes in the environment, affect most of the animals which are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Poikilothermic animals are animals whose body temperature changes with the surrounding environment. Unlike birds and mammals (which are warm-blooded and keep a constant body temperature), poikilotherms cannot regulate their internal temperature precisely.However, they do have some ways to cope with extreme heat or cold:Their tissues can adjust (for example, by changing osmotic pressure in cells).They can control blood flow to body parts: sending more blood to the skin to lose heat or keeping blood deeper inside to conserve heat.They change behavior to regulate temperature, like moving into the sun to warm up or into the shade to cool down.
Homeothermic animals are those whose body temperature remains constant irrespective of the variations in the temperature of the environment.
Aquatic animals have developed mechanisms for survival in harsh temperatures i.e: blubber etc
Desert living animals also adapt well to temperatures changes. For example they go into burrows in day and come out only during night. i.e: kangaroo rat.

questions.explanation: Poikilothermic animals are animals whose body temperature changes with the surrounding environment. Unlike birds and mammals (which are warm-blooded and keep a constant body temperature), poikilotherms cannot regulate their internal temperature precisely.However, they do have some ways to cope with extreme heat or cold:Their tissues can adjust (for example, by changing osmotic pressure in cells).They can control blood flow to body parts: sending more blood to the skin to lose heat or keeping blood deeper inside to conserve heat.They change behavior to regulate temperature, like moving into the sun to warm up or into the shade to cool down.

questions.text: Which of the following is not found in birds?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Pelvic girdle is present in birds.
Pectoral girdle is present in birds.
Hindlimbs are present in birds and help them to cover shorter distances in land.
Forelimbs are absent in birds because they have feathers instead of forelimbs to help them in flight.

questions.explanation: Forelimbs are absent in birds as they are modified into wings for flight. They are attached high on the back, to the anterior or thoracic region of the trunk, and are very powerful when compared with the size and strength of the bird. Each wing is elongated, flattened and distally pointed with its longitudinal axis at right angles to that of the trunk. The hindlimb is made of three parts i.e the thigh, shank and foot. The pectoral girdle on each side consists of three bones i.e a large coracoid, scapula and clavicle. The pelvic girdle consists of the ilium, ischium and pubis.

questions.text: Which group of animals have long hollow bones that are connected by air passages?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Reptiles have solid bones without significant air spaces, which provide strength for their terrestrial lifestyles.
While land vertebrates include various animals, they typically possess dense bones for support on land rather than hollow bones optimized for flight.
Birds (Class Aves) have long hollow bones connected by air passages, which reduce their weight and are a key adaptation for flight.
Mammals generally have solid bones, providing structural support necessary for their diverse lifestyles, but they do not have the lightweight adaptations seen in birds.

questions.explanation: The skeleton is light due to air spaces which is an adaptation for flying.

questions.text: Solenocytes are the main excretory structures in :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Echinoderms lack specialized excretory organs and so nitrogenous wastes chiefly in the form of ammonia, diffuses out through the respiratory surfaces.

Solenocytes are elongated, flagellated cells commonly found in lower invertebrates, such as flatworms (phylum Platyhelminthes), as well as in chordates (sub-phylum Cephalochordata) and several other animal species.
Nephridia are the excretory organs of annelids as in many invertebrates.
The excretory organ of Mollusca is metanephridia.

questions.explanation: Solenocytes, also called flame cells, are special excretory cells found in Platyhelminthes (flatworms). They are cup-shaped and contain bundles of tiny hair-like structures called cilia. The beating of these cilia looks like a flickering flame, which is why they are called flame cells.Their main function is excretion and osmoregulation—they help remove waste materials and maintain the balance of water and salts inside the body.

questions.text: The most appropriate term to describe the life cycle of Obelia is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Metamosphosis is usually a characteristic of phylum arthropoda and obelia is a cnidarian.
Neoteny in humans is the slowing or delaying of body development, compared to non-human primates, resulting in features such as a large head, a flat face, and relatively short arms.

Obelia belong to the Phylum Coelenterata. In Obelia, the lifecycle includes two clearly defined phases i.e a fixed polypoid phase (hydroid colony) and a pelagic medusoid phase. Hydroid colony has no gonads and reproduces by asexual budding to give rise to medusae. On the other hand, medusae reproduce exclusively a by sexual method (ova and sperms) to give rise to new hydroid colonies. This fact apparently seems to have given rise to the idea of alternation of generations, also called metagenesis.
This is incorrect.As one of them is correct.

questions.explanation: Obelia belong to the Phylum Coelenterata. In Obelia, the lifecycle includes two clearly defined phases i.e a fixed polypoid phase (hydroid colony) and a pelagic medusoid phase. Hydroid colony has no gonads and reproduces by asexual budding to give rise to medusae. On the other hand, medusae reproduce exclusively a by sexual method (ova and sperms) to give rise to new hydroid colonies. This fact apparently seems to have given rise to the idea of alternation of generations, also called metagenesis.

questions.text: The lower jaw in mammals is made up of?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The single bone that makes up the mammalian lower jaw is the dentary. At its posterior end is an articular (condyloid) process, which articulates with a bone called the squamosal in the upper jaw. The dentary also usually has an enlarged area at its posterior end, the coronoid process.
The maxilla is a bone which helps to make up the skull.
It is the dermal bone that is located posterior to the dentary and lateral to the articular and retroarticular bones of the lower jaw
The mandible, located inferiorly in the facial skeleton, is the largest and strongest bone of the face.

questions.explanation: The dentary is a type of membrane bone found in the lower jaw of vertebrates. Its main function is to hold and support the teeth.In most vertebrates, the lower jaw is made of several bones, and the dentary is just one of them.In mammals, however, the dentary is the only bone in the lower jaw. This is an important evolutionary feature that makes mammal jaws different from those of reptiles and amphibians.The dentary bone itself is short and comma-shaped in structure.

questions.text: Which one of the following cells found in the testes of rabbits secrete male hormones?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: These line the seminiferous tubules and act as a supporting covering, not for hormone secretion.
These are developing sperm cells (germ cells) that undergo meiosis to form spermatozoa.

Leydig's cells are responsible for secreting male hormones in the testes of rabbits.The connective tissue in between the seminiferous tubules of the testis contains special interstitial cells or cells of Leydig. They secrete the male sex hormones (androgens) such as testosterone. This stimulates the secondary sexual characteristics of the male such as the enlargement of the external genitals and accessory glands.
Found inside the seminiferous tubules.Their role is to nourish and support developing sperm and form the blood-testis barrier.

questions.explanation: The connective tissue in between the seminiferous tubules of the testis contains special interstitial cells or cells of Leydig. They secrete the male sex hormones (androgens) such as testosterone. This stimulates the secondary sexual characteristics of the male such as the enlargement of the external genitals and accessory glands.

questions.text: What is common among silverfish, scorpion, crab and honey bee?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Jointed legs are a distinctive characteristic of arthropods, which include silverfish, scorpions, crabs, and honey bees. This is the correct answer.
While metamorphosis occurs in some arthropods like the honey bee, it is not a feature common to all, such as the silverfish. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
Compound eyes are found in many arthropods, but not all of them have this feature, such as scorpions. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Poison glands are present in some arthropods like scorpions, but not all of them, such as honey bees and silverfish. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: All the mentioned animals—silverfish, scorpions, crabs, and honey bees—are part of the phylum Arthropoda, which is characterized by jointed appendages, including jointed legs. This is a fundamental feature of arthropods, making Option A the correct choice. Although some may have compound eyes, like honey bees, or undergo metamorphosis, these are not traits common to all listed animals. Similarly, while scorpions have poison glands, this is not a universal feature among the rest.

questions.text: The embryonated egg of Ascaris represents:

options.explanation: This option is not accurate for Ascaris eggs. Blastula is a stage of embryonic development characterized by a hollow ball of cells, and it is more typical in organisms like amphibians.
The embryonated egg of Ascaris represents an egg with a juvenile. Ascaris eggs undergo embryonic development, and when they are embryonated, they contain a developing juvenile worm inside.
The term “embryonated egg” itself implies that the egg has undergone some embryonic development. It doesn’t refer to a situation where one egg is inside another.
Gastrula is another stage of embryonic development characterized by the formation of germ layers. This term is not applicable to the embryonated egg of Ascaris.

questions.explanation: An embryonated egg of Ascaris is an egg that already contains a developing juvenile worm inside it.After fertilization, the zygote inside the egg begins to divide and develop (embryonic development).Over time, the embryo grows into a juvenile stage while still inside the protective egg shell.When the egg is embryonated, it means it is now infective because it carries a living juvenile worm ready to hatch once it enters the host’s body.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is without exception for sponges?

questions.text: When an animal has both the characters of male and female it is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Super female disorder is characterised by the presence of three X chromosomes.

Intersex is a general term used for a variety of situations in which a person is born with reproductive or sexual anatomy that doesn't fit the boxes of “female” or “male.”
Gynandromorph (hermaphrodite) is an animal that possesses both male and female characteristics. An example is the earthworm.

questions.explanation: Gynandromorph (hermaphrodite) is an animal that possesses both male and female characteristics. An example is the earthworm.

questions.text: Coelom is found between:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Body is also called endoderm and mesoderm is present between ectoderm and endoderm.
The mesoderm forms a loose cellular tissue called mesenchyma or parenchyma which ills the space between the ectoderm and endoderm.

The coelom is a fluid-filled cavity that serves as the main body cavity in vertebrates and many invertebrates. It is located between the body wall (ectoderm + mesoderm) and the gut (endoderm), and is completely lined by mesoderm.
Coelom is not present between mesoderm ans ectoderm.

questions.explanation: Coelom is a fluid filled cavity that forms the main body cavity of vertebrate and most invertebrate animals. It is found between the mesoderm and the body wall (endoderm).

questions.text: The pneumatic bone is found in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sharks do not have pneumatic bones. Sharks, along with other cartilaginous fish, have skeletons made of cartilage rather than bone. Pneumatic bones, characterized by air spaces, are a feature found in some birds to reduce their overall weight for flight.
Ranas do not possess pneumatic bones
Pneumatic bones are found in birds. These bones are characterized by having air spaces connected to the respiratory system, providing a lightweight structure that facilitates flight.
Whales do not possess pneumatic or hollow bones.

questions.explanation: The pneumatic bones in pigeons are hollow bones with air cavities that make the skeleton lightweight. This lightness is an adaptation for flight, as it reduces the overall body weight without reducing strength.

questions.text: The nephridia in earthworm are analogous to:

options.explanation: Nematoblasts of Hydra are organs of locomotion, food capture and anchorage.

Analogous organs are organs of very disparate organisms that are superficially similar but have evolved from vastly different origins. They have the same function but different structures. The nephridia in earthworm are analogous to flame cells of Planaria since both of them have excretory functions.
Gills and trachea are organs of respiration in prawn and insects respectively.
Gills and trachea are organs of respiration in prawn and insects respectively.

questions.text: Which of the following is common among mammals?

options.explanation: Mammals do not undergo the process of moulting, which is the periodic shedding of the outer covering (like skin, feathers, or exoskeleton) seen in many other animals.

This is not common to all.
This is not common to all mammals.

questions.explanation: Mammals do not undergo moulting. Moulting is usually exhibited by invertebrates. questions.text: The formation of a canal system in sponges is due to:

options.explanation: Canal system in sponges develops due to folding of body wall. Three types of canal systems in sponges are ascon type, sycon type and leucon type. Ascon type of canal system is most primitive and shows minimal foldingSo, the correct answer is 'Folding of inner walls'
