After the last molt of a cockroach nymph, there are several external changes as it transitions into the adult stage. However, the anal cerci, which are sensory appendages located at the end of the abdomen, generally don't undergo dramatic changes during this transition.

questions.explanation: Cockroaches undergo paurometabolous development. The nymph moults about 67 times to reach the adult form. The next to last nymphal stage has wing pads but only adult cockroaches have wings.

questions.text: Select the correct option with respect to cockroaches.
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Malpighian tubules convert nitrogenous wastes into urea
options.explanation: Malpighian tubules are the main excretory structures in cockroach. They extract nitrogenous wastes and water from haemolymph and reabsorb certain salts resulting in the precipitation of uric acid. So, cockroach is uricotelic.

options.text: Males bear short anal styles not present in females
options.explanation: Males have paired anal styles on 9th abdominal sternite which are absent in females.

options.text: Nervous system comprises of a dorsal nerve cord and ten pairs of ganglia
options.explanation: The nervous system of a cockroach does not exactly comprise a dorsal nerve cord and ten pairs of ganglia. Instead, cockroaches, like other insects, have a ventral nerve cord and a series of ganglia.

options.text: The forewings are tegmina which are used in flight
options.explanation: The forewings of a cockroach are not used for flight. In cockroaches, the forewings are called tegmina, and they serve a protective function. Tegmina are thick, leathery, and serve to cover and protect the hindwings, which are the wings used for actual flight. Cockroaches are capable fliers, and they use their hindwings for flying.

questions.explanation: Males have paired anal styles on 9th abdominal sternite which are absent in females.

questions.text: Select the correct statement from the ones given below with respect to Periplaneta americana:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Nervous system located is dorsally and consists of segmentally arranged ganglia joined by a pair of longitudinal connectives.
options.explanation: In cockroach, the nervous system is located ventrally, not dorsally.It does consist of segmentally arranged ganglia connected by paired longitudinal connectives, but since it is ventral, this statement is incorrect.

options.text: Males bear a pair of short thread like anal styles.
options.explanation: Male cockroaches possess a pair of short, thread-like anal styles — small appendages found below the anal cerci.Females lack these anal styles.Hence, this statement is correct.

options.text: There are 16 very long Malpighian tubules present at the junctions of the midgut and hindgut.
options.explanation: In Periplaneta americana, there are about 100–150 yellow-colored Malpighian tubules, not 16.They are found at the junction of midgut and hindgut, but the number given is wrong.

options.text: Grinding of food is carried out only by the mouth parts.
options.explanation: In cockroach, grinding occurs not only by mouthparts (mandibles) but also by the gizzard (proventriculus) in the foregut.The gizzard has chitinous teeth that further grind the food.So, saying “only by mouthparts” is wrong.

questions.explanation: Nutrition The posterior segment of cockroaches bears appendages called anal cerci. These are found in both male and female, but male cockroaches can be distinguished by female ones by the presence of an extra pair of accessory appendages called anal styles. These assist during copulation.

questions.text: One very special feature in the earthworm (Pheretima) is that:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Fertilization of eggs occurs inside the body
options.explanation: In earthworms, fertilization actually occurs externally. Earthworms are not true hermaphrodites; they have separate sexes.

options.text: The typhlosole greatly increases the effective absorption area of the digested food in the intestine
options.explanation: In Pheretima, the intestine is next to the stomach. It is a long, wide and thin-walled tube extending from the 15th segment to the last. The second or middle part of the intestine lies between the 27th segment up to the 23–25th segments in front of the anus. This is characterised by the presence of a highly glandular and vascular longitudinal ridge, arising as a median ingrowth of the dorsal aspect of the intestinal cavity. This is called the typhlosole. The typhlosole greatly increases the effective absorption area of the digested food in the intestine.

options.text: The S-shaped setae embedded in the integument are the defensive weapons used against the enemies
options.explanation: The S-shaped setae (bristle-like structures) in earthworms are not primarily defensive weapons against enemies. Instead, these setae play a crucial role in locomotion and anchoring the worm in the soil.

options.text: It has a long dorsal tubular heart
options.explanation: Earthworm does not have a dorsal tubular heart. Earthworms have a simple, tubular circulatory system, but their hearts are muscular vessels called aortic arches.

questions.explanation: In Pheretima, the intestine is next to the stomach. It is a long, wide and thin-walled tube extending from the 15th segment to the last. The second or middle part of the intestine lies between the 27th segment up to the 23–25th segments in front of the anus. This is characterised by the presence of a highly glandular and vascular longitudinal ridge, arising as a median ingrowth of the dorsal aspect of the intestinal cavity. This is called the typhlosole. The typhlosole greatly increases the effective absorption area of the digested food in the intestine.

questions.text: Which of the following happens is true about the common cockroach?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Malpighian tubules are its excretory organs projecting out from the colon
options.explanation: Malpighian tubules are indeed excretory organs in insects, including cockroaches. However, they do not project directly from the colon. Instead, Malpighian tubules are blind-ended tubes that are attached to the junction of the midgut and hindgut in insects. These tubules play a crucial role in removing nitrogenous waste and other substances from the hemolymph, and the waste is then excreted as solid material.

options.text: Oxygen is transported by haemoglobin in their blood
options.explanation: No, in insects like cockroaches, oxygen is not primarily transported by hemoglobin in the blood. Instead, insects use a respiratory system called tracheal respiration. They have a network of tubes called tracheae that directly deliver oxygen to the body's cells.

options.text: Its nitrogenous excretory product is urea
options.explanation: Its nitrogenous excretory product is solid uric acid.

options.text: Their food is ground by mandibles and the gizzard
options.explanation: The mouth part of the cockroach contains two mandibles. When both the mandibles work simultaneously in the horizontal plane, the food matter is cut and masticated into fine and smaller pieces. The gizzard is a part of the alimentary canal. It bears six muscular folds which are covered by chitinous conical plates, the teeth, used for grinding the food.

questions.explanation: The mouth part of the cockroach contains two mandibles. When both the mandibles work simultaneously in the horizontal plane, the food matter is cut and masticated into fine and smaller pieces. The gizzard is a part of the alimentary canal. It bears six muscular folds which are covered by chitinous conical plates, the teeth, used for grinding the food.

questions.text: Frogs differ from humans because they possess:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Both frogs and humans have paired cerebral hemispheres in their brains. The cerebral hemispheres are the largest part of the brain and are divided into left and right hemispheres.

Both frogs and humans have a hepatic portal system. The hepatic portal system is a network of blood vessels that directs blood from the digestive organs, such as the stomach and intestines, to the liver before it returns to the heart.
Human erythrocytes are enucleated and discoidal while in frogs erythrocytes are large, oval and biconvex nucleated cells. Erythrocytes are the carriers of haemoglobin.

options.text: A thyroid as well as parathyroid
options.explanation: Both frogs and humans have a thyroid gland and parathyroid glands.

questions.explanation: Human erythrocytes are enucleated and discoidal while in frogs erythrocytes are large, oval and biconvex nucleated cells. Erythrocytes are the carriers of haemoglobin.

questions.text: Which one of the following structures in Pheretima is correctly matched with its function?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In a mature earthworm, the 14th and 16th segments are covered by a prominent dark band of glandular tissues called clitellum which secretes cocoon where fertilization and development takes place. In alimentary canal,the muscular gizzard (8th and 9th segments) helps in grinding the soil particles and decaying leaves. The characteristic feature of the intestine between 26th and 35th segments is the presence of an internal median fold of dorsal wall called typhlosole. This increases the effective area of absorption in the intestine. In each body segment, except the first, last and clitellum, there are rows of S shaped setae, embedded in the epidermal pits in the middle of each segment. Setae can be extended or retracted. Their principal role is in locomotion.

The gizzard in earthworms (such as Pheretima) is not involved in the absorption of digested food. The gizzard is a muscular structure located in the digestive system, and its main function is mechanical digestion.

Setae, which are bristle-like structures on the body of earthworms like Pheretima, are primarily involved in locomotion and burrowing rather than defense against predators. Earthworms use their setae to anchor themselves in the soil and facilitate movement through peristaltic contractions.

The typhlosole is not primarily involved in the storage of extra nutrients in earthworms, including Pheretima. The typhlosole is a structural fold in the intestine of certain earthworms, and its main function is to increase the surface area for absorption of nutrients.

questions.explanation: In a mature earthworm, the 14th and 16th segments are covered by a prominent dark band of glandular tissues called clitellum which secretes cocoon where fertilization and development takes place. In alimentary canal,the muscular gizzard (8th and 9th segments) helps in grinding the soil particles and decaying leaves. The characteristic feature of the intestine between 26th and 35th segments is the presence of an internal median fold of dorsal wall called typhlosole. This increases the effective area of absorption in the intestine. In each body segment, except the first, last and clitellum, there are rows of S shaped setae, embedded in the epidermal pits in the middle of each segment. Setae can be extended or retracted. Their principal role is in locomotion.

questions.text: Which one of the following correctly describes the location of some body parts in the earthworm Pheretima?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Four pairs of spermathecae in the 4th and 7th segments
options.explanation: The most common arrangement is two pairs of spermathecae in segments 4 and 5.

options.text: One pair of ovaries attached at the intersegmental septum of the 14th and 15th segments
options.explanation: Earthworms typically have a pair of ovaries, but they are located in the 12th and 13th segments, not the 14th and 15th segments.

options.text: Two pairs of testes in the 10th and 11th segments
options.explanation: In the Pheretima, two pairs of the testis sac are situated in the tenth and eleventh segments. Each testis sac of the tenth segment encloses a testis and a seminal funnel. Each testis sac of the eleventh segment encloses a testis, a seminal vesicle and a seminal funnel.

options.text: Two pairs of accessory glands in the 16th and 18th segments
options.explanation: Earthworms typically have four pairs of accessory glands located in segments 14, 15, 17, and 19. These glands play a role in producing the mucous and cocoon materials during reproduction. The 16th and 18th segments are not typically associated with the presence of accessory glands in the reproductive system of earthworms.

questions.explanation: In the Pheretima, two pairs of the testis sac are situated in the tenth and eleventh segments. Each testis sac of the tenth segment encloses a testis and a seminal funnel. Each testis sac of the eleventh segment encloses a testis, a seminal vesicle and a seminal funnel.

questions.text: The kind of tissue that forms the supportive structure in our pinna(external ears) is also found in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Nails are made of keratin, a hard protein not cartilage at all.So, nails have no elastic cartilage.
Ear ossicles (malleus, incus, stapes) are tiny bones found in the middle ear.They are made of bone tissue, not cartilage.So, not the same tissue as in the pinna.

Yellow elastic fibrocartilage, a type of skeletal tissue, is found in the pinna, Eustachian tubes, epiglottis and tip of the nose. It is a type of cartilage and due to presence of yellow fibres, it becomes more flexible.
Vertebrae are made of bone tissue.Between vertebrae are fibrocartilage discs, not elastic cartilage.So, this is a different kind of cartilage.

questions.explanation: Yellow elastic fibrocartilage, a type of skeletal tissue, is found in the pinna, Eustachian tubes, epiglottis and tip of the nose. It is a type of cartilage and due to presence of yellow fibres, it becomes more flexible.

questions.text: Earthworms have no skeleton but during burrowing, the anterior end becomes turgid and acts as a hydraulic skeleton.This is due to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gut peristalsis is the wave-like movement of the alimentary canal that helps move food forward.It has nothing to do with the hydraulic skeleton or burrowing movement.

Setae are tiny bristle-like structures on each segment.They anchor the worm in the soil during movement but do not create turgidity or pressure.They help in traction, not in forming a hydrostatic skeleton.

The hydraulic skeleton is the system of support found in soft-bodied invertebrates which relies on the incompressibility of fluids contained within the body cavity. In earthworms the coelomic fluid is under pressure within the coelom and therefore provides support for internal organs. Due to the hydraulic skeleton, during burrowing, the anterior end becomes turgid and aids in the relaxation of longitudinal muscles.
Blood in earthworms is confined to blood vessels (closed circulatory system).It functions in transport of gases and nutrients, not in support or rigidity.So, it does not form the hydraulic skeleton.

questions.explanation: The hydraulic skeleton is the system of support found in soft-bodied invertebrates which relies on the incompressibility of fluids contained within the body cavity. In earthworms the coelomic fluid is under pressure within the coelom and therefore provides support for internal organs. Due to the hydraulic skeleton, during burrowing, the anterior end becomes turgid and aids in the relaxation of longitudinal muscles.

questions.text: Which one of the following is the true description about an animal concerned?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Rat: Left kidney is slightly higher in position than the right one
options.explanation: The kidney position in rats is a minor anatomical detail not a defining characteristic of rats.In contrast, the number and arrangement of spiracles is a distinctive and diagnostic feature of cockroaches it clearly identifies their respiratory system and body segmentation.

options.text: Cockroach: 10 pairs of spiracles(2 pairs on thorax and 8 pairs on abdomen)
options.explanation: There are 10 pairs of spiracles in cockroach. Two pairs are thoracic in which first pair is known as mesothoracic, lying infront of the mesothorax between the bases of first and second pair of legs and are the largest. The second pair is called metathoracic. Abdominal spiracles are eight pairs. The first pair is dorsal in position and lies on the lateral margins of the first abdominal tergum. The remaining are situated on the sides of their corresponding segments on the pleura between the terga and sterna. Spiracles are meant for intake of fresh air and release of foul air.

options.text: Earthworm: The alimentary canal consists of a sequence of pharynx, oesophagus, stomach, gizzard and intestine.
options.explanation: The earthworm has the following correct sequence:Mouth → Buccal cavity → Pharynx → Oesophagus → Crop → Gizzard → Intestine → AnusThere is no true “stomach” in earthworm.

options.text: Frog: Body divisible into three regions: head, neck and trunk.
options.explanation: Frog’s body is divided into two regions only:Head and trunk.There is no neck and no tail in adult frogs.

questions.explanation: There are 10 pairs of spiracles in cockroach. Two pairs are thoracic in which first pair is known as mesothoracic, lying infront of the mesothorax between the bases of first and second pair of legs and are the largest. The second pair is called metathoracic. Abdominal spiracles are eight pairs. The first pair is dorsal in position and lies on the lateral margins of the first abdominal tergum. The remaining are situated on the sides of their corresponding segments on the pleura between the terga and sterna. Spiracles are meant for intake of fresh air and release of foul air.

questions.text: The blood of Pheretima is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Haemocyanin is a blue, copper-containing respiratory pigment, found in arthropods and molluscs (e.g. prawns, snails).Pheretima is an annelid, not an arthropod.It does not have haemocyanin at all, and its blood is red, not blue.
Same reason as above earthworms have no haemocyanin, and their blood is red, not blue.So, this option is wrong for both color and pigment.

The blood of Pheretima is red due to haemoglobin, but the haemoglobin is not inside corpuscles (cells).Earthworm blood lacks red blood cells.Their haemoglobin is dissolved in plasma instead.So, this statement is partly true (red, haemoglobin) but wrong location (not in corpuscles).

The circulatory or blood vascular system of earthworm is a closed system consisting of blood vessels and capillaries which ramify all parts of the body. Blood is composed of fluid plasma and colourless corpuscles, physiologically comparable to the leucocytes of the vertebrates. The red respiratory pigment, haemoglobin (or erythrocruorin) occurs dissolved in plasma. It gives a red colour to blood and aids in the transportation of oxygen for respiration.

questions.explanation: The circulatory or blood vascular system of earthworm is a closed system consisting of blood vessels and capillaries which ramify all parts of the body. Blood is composed of fluid plasma and colourless corpuscles, physiologically comparable to the leucocytes of the vertebrates. The red respiratory pigment, haemoglobin (or erythrocruorin) occurs dissolved in plasma. It gives a red colour to blood and aids in the transportation of oxygen for respiration.

questions.text: Pheretima posthuma are highly useful because:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Their burrows make the soil loose
options.explanation: It’s true that earthworms make burrows which help loosen the soil, improving aeration and drainage.However, that’s not the only reason they are highly useful — their castings and mixing of organic matter are also key ecological benefits.

options.text: They make the soil porous, leave their castings and take organic debris in the soil
options.explanation: Pheretima Posthuma are highly useful and beneficial in agriculture. Their habit of burrowing and swallowing earth makes it porous and increases the soil fertility in many ways. Their burrows permit penetration of air and moisture in porous soil and their excretory wastes and other secretions also enrich soil by adding nitrogenous matters to the soil. Pheretima posthuma is not used as fish meal. Whereas a small white earthworm (Enchytraeus albidus) is often grown in soil and used to feed aquarium fish.

options.text: They are used as fish meal
options.explanation: While earthworms can be used as fish bait, their main usefulness in ecology is soil fertility, not as fish meal.This is not their major ecological or economic importance.

options.text: They kill the birds due to biomagnification of chlorinated hydrocarbons
options.explanation: This statement is completely false.Earthworms do not kill birds in fact, they are beneficial to soil and the ecosystem.Biomagnification of chlorinated hydrocarbons (like DDT) affects birds of prey, not because of earthworms’ normal activity, but due to human pesticide use.

questions.explanation: Pheretima Posthuma are highly useful and beneficial in agriculture. Their habit of burrowing and swallowing earth makes it porous and increases the soil fertility in many ways. Their burrows permit penetration of air and moisture in porous soil and their excretory wastes and other secretions also enrich soil by adding nitrogenous matters to the soil. Pheretima posthuma is not used as fish meal. Whereas a small white earthworm (Enchytraeus albidus) is often grown in soil and used to feed aquarium fish.

questions.text: Photoreceptors of the earthworm occur on their:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The clitellum is a reproductive structure, a thickened glandular band involved in cocoon formation.It has no sensory or photoreceptive function.
Earthworms do not have eyes — not even simple ones.They have no eye structures, only light-sensitive cells in their skin.So this option is completely wrong.

Photoreceptors are restricted only to the dorsal surface. They are more numerous on the prostomium and peristomium of the earthworm and gradually reduce in number towards the posterior end of the body. They are totally absent in the clitellum. Each photoreceptor consists of a single ovoid cell, with a nucleus and clear cytoplasm containing a network of neurofibrillae and a small transparent L shaped lens or optic organelle made up of a hyaline substance. Photoreceptors enable worms to judge the intensity and duration of light.

Although sensory cells are found on the body surface, the light-sensitive photoreceptors are concentrated dorsally, not laterally.Lateral areas may have tactile or chemoreceptive cells, but not the main photoreceptors.

questions.explanation: Photoreceptors are restricted only to the dorsal surface. They are more numerous on the prostomium and peristomium of the earthworm and gradually reduce in number towards the posterior end of the body. They are totally absent in the clitellum. Each photoreceptor consists of a single ovoid cell, with a nucleus and clear cytoplasm containing a network of neurofibrillae and a small transparent L shaped lens or optic organelle made up of a hyaline substance. Photoreceptors enable worms to judge the intensity and duration of light.

questions.text: All are the features of kingdom animalia except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Animals are heterotrophic, meaning they cannot perform photosynthesis.They depend on other organisms for food (plants or animals).

Organic synthesis is not exhibited by kingdo animalia. Organic synthesis is referred to as photosynthesis in which inorganic molecules (water and carbon dioxide) are converted into organic molecule (Glucose). This process takes place in Autotrophs.
All animals are eukaryotic, meaning their cells have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.
All animals are multicellular organisms.Even the simplest animals (like sponges) are composed of many specialized cells.

questions.explanation: Organic synthesis is not exhibited by kingdom animalia. Organic synthesis is referred to as photosynthesis in which inorganic molecules (water and carbon dioxide) are converted into organic molecule (Glucose). This process takes place in Autotrophs.

questions.text: A feature shared by all animals:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Not all animals are motile. For example, sponges remain fixed in one place.
Animals are found in both aquatic and terrestrial environments, so this is not a feature shared by all animals.
Many animals, such as invertebrates, move without limbs. Thus, this is not a universal feature.
All animals are heterotrophs, meaning they obtain their food from other organisms. This is a defining feature of the kingdom Animalia.

questions.explanation: All animals are heterotrophs, which means they rely on consuming other organisms for nutrition. This is a fundamental trait that distinguishes them from autotrophs, like plants, which can produce their own food through photosynthesis. The other options are not universal among animals: some animals, such as sponges, are sessile and not motile; animals inhabit both aquatic and terrestrial environments, so an aquatic lifestyle is not a shared trait; and not all animals have limbs, as many invertebrates move in other ways.

questions.text: Animals of grade radiata are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Correct! Grade radiata animals, such as those in the phylum Cnidaria, are diploblastic, meaning they develop from two primary germ layers: the ectoderm and endoderm.
Incorrect. Acoelomate refers to animals without a body cavity, which is not a defining characteristic of grade radiata animals.
Incorrect. Triploblastic animals develop from three germ layers, which is characteristic of grade bilateria, not radiata.
Incorrect. Pseudocoelomate refers to animals with a body cavity that is not completely lined by mesoderm, which is not relevant to the grade radiata classification.

questions.explanation: Grade radiata animals, such as those in the phylum Cnidaria, are diploblastic, meaning they develop from two primary germ layers: the ectoderm and endoderm.

questions.text: Reproductive system arises from:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is incorrect. 'Germlayer' is a generic term for any of the primary layers of cells in an embryo, but it doesn't specify which one forms the reproductive system.
This is incorrect. The ectoderm primarily forms the outermost layer of the skin and the nervous system, not the reproductive system.
This is correct. The mesoderm is responsible for forming many systems, including the reproductive system, muscles, and the circulatory system.
This is incorrect. The endoderm mainly forms the lining of the digestive and respiratory systems.

questions.explanation: In triploblastic animals, the body develops from three primary germ layers: ectoderm, mesoderm, and endoderm. The ectoderm forms structures such as the skin and the nervous system. The endoderm gives rise to the lining of the digestive and respiratory systems. The mesoderm forms the reproductive system and other structures such as muscles and the circulatory system. Therefore, the reproductive system arises from the mesoderm, making Option C the correct choice.

questions.text: Pseudocoelom develops from:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A blastostyle is a structure found in some invertebrates, particularly hydrozoans, which are part of the phylum Cnidaria. The blastostyle gives rise to medusae, which are jellyfish-like individuals in the life cycle of hydrozoans. Medusae, in turn, release gametes for sexual reproduction, leading to the formation of new colonies through a combination of sexual and asexual reproduction in the hydrozoan life cycle.
The body cavity that is formed from Blastocoel (cavity that is formed at blastula stage of developing embryo) is called pseudocoelom.
This is incorrect.A blastocyst is an early stage of embryo development in mammals (including humans).It forms about 5–6 days after fertilization and is the stage that implants into the uterus.
Mesoderm gives rise to reproductive system.

questions.explanation: The body cavity that is formed from Blastocoel (cavity that is formed at blastula stage of developing embryo) is called pseudocoelom.

questions.text: Reproductive system, excretory system, circulatory system and respiratory system developed from:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The ectoderm primarily forms structures like the skin and nervous system, not the internal systems like the circulatory or respiratory systems.
The endoderm mainly forms the lining of the digestive and respiratory tracts, not the excretory or circulatory systems.
The mesoderm is responsible for forming major internal systems such as the reproductive, excretory, circulatory, and respiratory systems in triploblastic animals.
While these layers form essential parts of an organism, they do not collectively form the internal systems mentioned in the question.

questions.explanation: The mesoderm is responsible for forming many systems, including the reproductive system, muscles, and the circulatory system.

questions.text: Bilaterally symmetrical animals are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Bilateral symmetry actually represents higher organization compared to radial or asymmetrical forms.It is found in more advanced animals, not lesser developed ones.

Animals lacking mesoderm are diploblastic (having only ectoderm and endoderm).Example: Cnidarians (Hydra, Jellyfish) these are radially symmetrical, not bilateral.Bilaterally symmetrical animals are triploblastic, meaning they have three germ layers: ectoderm, mesoderm, and endoderm.
Bilateral symmetry is found in many phyla, not just one.Examples:Platyhelminthes (flatworms)Annelida (earthworms)Arthropoda (insects)Mollusca, Chordata, etc.
All bilaterally symmetrical animals are triploblastic.

questions.explanation: All bilaterally symmetrical animals are triploblastic.

questions.text: In coelomate the layer that surround endoderm is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The coelomic epithelium is formed by mesodermal cells that line the coelom.However, the question specifically asks which layer surrounds the endoderm, not just any lining of the coelom.The coelomic epithelium exists on both sides, so this term is too general.
Ectoderm is the outermost germ layer, forming the body covering, nervous system, etc.It lies outside the mesoderm, far from the endoderm.

All bilateral symmetrical animals are triploblastic. Coelom (body cavity) is formed due to splitting of mesoderm at embryonic stages. Its outer layer is called parietal layer which underlines the body wall, while inner layer of mesoderm is called visceral layer that surrounds the endoderm.
The parietal (somatic) mesoderm lines the body wall and is in contact with the ectoderm, not the endoderm.It forms muscles of the body wall and dermis of the skin.

questions.explanation: In coelomates, the mesoderm splits to form the coelom. The visceral layer of mesoderm surrounds the endoderm (gut), while the parietal layer lines the body wall.

questions.text: All are correct about acoelomates except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Acoelomates possess a sac-like digestive system, which is a simple digestive cavity.
Acoelomates do not have a well-developed respiratory system. These animals, for example, those in Phylum Platyhelminthes, rely on diffusion through their body surface for gaseous exchange.
Acoelomates have a body cavity filled with parenchyma, a type of tissue that supports the body structure and fills the space between organs.
Acoelomates possess a relatively simple excretory system, typically consisting of protonephridia, not a well-developed one.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: Well-developed respiratory system. Acoelomates, such as those in Phylum Platyhelminthes, lack a well-developed respiratory system and rely on diffusion through their body surface for gas exchange. Option A is incorrect because acoelomates have a sac-like digestive system. Option C is incorrect as the body cavity of acoelomates is filled with parenchyma. Option D is incorrect because their excretory system is simple, consisting of structures like protonephridia, rather than being well-developed.

questions.text: A body cavity is absent in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Acoelomates lack a body cavity, or coelom, between the body wall and the digestive system. This group includes the phylum Platyhelminthes, known as flatworms.
Pseudocoelomates possess a pseudocoelom, a body cavity that is not fully lined with mesoderm. Examples include the phylum Nematoda (roundworms).
This option is incorrect because pseudocoelomates do possess a type of body cavity, although it is not a true coelom.
Coelomates possess a true coelom, a body cavity fully lined with mesoderm. This group includes most animals, such as annelids, mollusks, and vertebrates.

questions.explanation: Acoelomates lack a body cavity, or coelom, between the body wall and the digestive system. This group includes the phylum Platyhelminthes, known as flatworms.

questions.text: Which of the following are pseudocoelomates?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Flatworms are acoelomates, meaning no body cavity.They have solid bodies with only tissues between gut and body wall.So, they are not pseudocoelomates.
Echinoderms (starfish, sea urchins) are triploblastic coelomates.They have a true coelom, completely lined by mesoderm.Not pseudocoelomates.
Molluscs (snails, clams, squids) are triploblastic coelomates.Their body cavity (coelom) is well-developed and mesoderm-lined.So, they are not pseudocoelomates.
Aschelminthes (roundworms, nematodes) are pseudocoelomates.They have a body cavity called pseudocoel, partially lined by mesoderm.This cavity acts as a hydrostatic skeleton aiding in movement.

questions.explanation: Aschelminthes (roundworms, nematodes) are pseudocoelomates.They have a body cavity called pseudocoel, partially lined by mesoderm.This cavity acts as a hydrostatic skeleton aiding in movement.

questions.text: A fluid filled cavity which is mesodermal in origin but absent in nematodes is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The coelom is a true body cavity that is completely lined by mesodermal tissue. It is absent in nematodes, which instead possess a pseudocoelom.
The gastrocoel is the primitive gut cavity formed during embryonic development, not a body cavity related to mesodermal tissue.
A pseudocoelom is a body cavity that is not entirely lined by mesodermal tissue. Nematodes possess a pseudocoelom, which is why this option is incorrect.
The spongocoel is the central cavity found in sponges, unrelated to mesodermal origins or nematodes.

questions.explanation: The coelom is a true body cavity that is entirely lined by mesodermal tissue, providing space for the development and suspension of organs. Nematodes, however, are classified as pseudocoelomates, meaning their body cavity is not completely lined by mesoderm. This distinguishes them from coelomates, which have a fully mesoderm-lined cavity. Other options like the gastrocoel and spongocoel refer to different types of cavities unrelated to the mesodermal origin, while the pseudocoelom, although a body cavity, lacks a complete mesodermal lining and is present in nematodes.

questions.text: Everything is true about coelom except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Found between body wall and gut
options.explanation: The coelom lies between the outer body wall (ectoderm) and the gut wall (endoderm).This is the main definition of a coelom.
The coelomic cavity contains coelomic fluid, which helps in:Transport of nutrients and gases.Providing a hydrostatic skeleton in some animals.

The key feature of a true coelom is that it is completely lined by mesodermal epithelium (called peritoneum).That’s why animals with a true coelom are called coelomates (like annelids, mollusks, arthropods, echinoderms, chordates).

options.text: Bound internally by cuticle of intestine
options.explanation: Coelom is internally lined by visceral layer of mesoderm. In pseudocoelomates body cavity is internally bound by cuticle of intestine.

questions.explanation: Coelom is internally lined by visceral layer of mesoderm. In pseudocoelomates body cavity is internally bound by cuticle of intestine.

questions.text: Acoelomates do not lack:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Acoelomates lack a coelom.That’s the main defining feature of acoelomates.
Acoelomates are triploblastic animals in which all the three germinal layers ectoderm. mesoderm and endoderm are present.
Acoelomates do not have a specialized transport system.Transport occurs directly via diffusion because of their thin, flat bodies.
Acoelomates do not have a coelom, hence no coelomic fluid.

questions.explanation: Acoelomates are triploblastic animals in which all the three germinal layers ectoderm. mesoderm and endoderm are present.

questions.text: In acoelomates, gut is lined externally by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The visceral mesoderm is a layer of the mesoderm in embryonic development that surrounds and supports the internal organs or viscera, including the heart, lungs, and digestive organs. It plays a vital role in the formation and development of these organs during embryogenesis, providing structural and functional support to ensure proper organ function.

The parietal mesoderm is another layer of the mesoderm that forms during early embryonic development. It lines the body cavity walls and is involved in creating the connective tissues, bones, and muscles of the limbs and trunk, contributing to the overall body structure.

The splanchnic mesoderm is a layer of the mesoderm that develops during embryogenesis. It surrounds the endoderm-derived organs, such as the heart, blood vessels, and portions of the digestive and respiratory systems, playing a crucial role in their formation and support.

The endoderm is one of the three primary germ layers in early embryonic development, giving rise to the innermost tissue layer in animals. It forms the lining of the digestive tract and respiratory system and contributes to various internal organs like the liver, pancreas, and lungs.

questions.explanation: The visceral mesoderm is a layer of the mesoderm in embryonic development that surrounds and supports the internal organs or viscera, including the heart, lungs, and digestive organs. It plays a vital role in the formation and development of these organs during embryogenesis, providing structural and functional support to ensure proper organ function.

questions.text: Which system is more developed in acoelomates?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Acoelomate has sac type digestive system, no respiratory and osmoregulatory systems. They have tubular excretory system called protonephridia having flame cells.
Acoelomates lack respiratory organs.Gas exchange occurs directly through the body surface by diffusion, so respiratory system is not developed.
Many acoelomates (like Planaria) have incomplete or branched gut, no anus.Digestive system is less developed compared to excretory system.
The excretory system (protonephridia) also performs osmoregulation, but the main system is the excretory system.So osmoregulation is not a separate system, it’s a function of the excretory system.

questions.explanation: Acoelomate has sac type digestive system, no respiratory and osmoregulatory systems. They have tubular excretory system called protonephridia having flame cells.

questions.text: Spiral and determinate type cleavage is present in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Correct! Pinworms are nematodes, which are classified as protostomes. They exhibit spiral and determinate cleavage during embryonic development.
Incorrect. Starfish are echinoderms, which are deuterostomes. They have radial and indeterminate cleavage.
Incorrect. Cake urchins are also echinoderms and exhibit radial and indeterminate cleavage typical of deuterostomes.
Incorrect. Amphioxus is a chordate, which places it in the deuterostome category. It exhibits radial and indeterminate cleavage.

questions.explanation: Spiral and determinate cleavage is a feature of protostomes, a group that includes organisms such as annelids, mollusks, and nematodes. Pinworms, being nematodes, fall into this category and exhibit this type of embryonic development. In contrast, starfish, cake urchins, and amphioxus are deuterostomes, which are characterized by radial and indeterminate cleavage. This fundamental difference in early developmental stages is key to differentiating between these two major groups of animals.

questions.text: Indeterminate cleavage is not a characteristic of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Echinoderms are deuterostomes.They exhibit indeterminate cleavage, meaning each early cell can develop into a complete embryo.So, this is not the answer.
Molluscs are proterostomes and have determinate cleavage in which fate of blastomeres is foretold. Indeterminate cleavage is characteristic of deuterostomes.
Chordates are deuterostomes.They exhibit indeterminate cleavage.So, this is not the answer.
Hemichordates are also deuterostomes.They exhibit indeterminate cleavage.So, this is not the answer.

questions.explanation: Molluscs are proterostomes and have determinate cleavage in which fate of blastomeres is foretold. Indeterminate cleavage is characteristic of deuterostomes.

questions.text: It is not true for Phylum Platyhelminthes:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is incorrect because Phylum Platyhelminthes exhibit bilateral symmetry, meaning they have a single plane of symmetry dividing the body into two mirrored halves.
This is incorrect because members of Phylum Platyhelminthes are triploblastic, having three germ layers: ectoderm, mesoderm, and endoderm.
This is incorrect because Platyhelminthes have a dorsoventrally flattened body, which is a characteristic feature of this phylum.
This is correct. Phylum Platyhelminthes have bilateral symmetry, not radial symmetry, which is found in organisms like jellyfish and starfish.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Radially symmetrical body'. Phylum Platyhelminthes is characterized by bilateral symmetry, meaning there is only one plane of symmetry that divides the body into left and right halves. In contrast, radial symmetry, which is incorrect for this phylum, involves multiple planes of symmetry that pass through a central axis, as seen in organisms like cnidarians. Additionally, Platyhelminthes are triploblastic metazoans with a dorsoventrally compressed body, which further distinguishes them from radially symmetrical organisms.

questions.text: The body of Aschelminthes is covered by a hard layer of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chitin is found in arthropod exoskeletons (e.g., insects, crustaceans) and some fungi.Aschelminthes do not have chitin in their body covering.
Intestinal parasites, generally do not absorb nutrients through the body surface as there is usually a thick cuticle to protect themselves from the host's digestive liquids.
Cilia are hair-like projections used for movement or feeding.They are present in some protozoans or larval stages of some animals, but not on the adult body of Aschelminthes.
Spongin is a protein found in sponges (Porifera), forming their skeletal fibers.It is not present in nematodes or Aschelminthes.

questions.explanation: Intestinal parasites, generally do not absorb nutrients through the body surface as there is usually a thick cuticle to protect themselves from the host's digestive liquids.

questions.text: _is a human parasite commonly known as pinworm.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is a parasite known as the liver fluke. It infects the liver, causing a disease called fascioliasis. Humans can get infected by consuming water or plants contaminated with the parasite's larvae.

This is the pork tapeworm. When ingested, typically through undercooked pork, it can cause a condition called taeniasis. Its larvae can also cause cysticercosis, a more severe condition affecting the muscles, brain, and other organs.
Enterobius vermicularis commonly known as pinworm is cosmopolitan but more common in Europe and America. Pinworms are parasites in the human caecum, colon, and appendix.
This is not a parasite. It's the scientific name for the moon jellyfish, a type of jellyfish found in oceans worldwide. It's not a parasite but rather a free-swimming marine animal.

questions.explanation: Enterobius vermicularis commonly known as pinworm is cosmopolitan but more common in Europe and America. Pinworms are parasites in the human caecum, colon, and appendix.

questions.text: The nervous system in Aschelninthes consists of a nerve ring that encircles the following:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The nerve system surrounds the entry for ingesting food, aiding in sensing and controlling feeding behaviors.
The Nervous system consists of a nerve ring around the esophagus (pharynx) from which the nerve cord and fibres extend in various directions.
Hypothetical in these invertebrates, might control specialized movements or functions in a similar anatomical structure.
Nerve concentration around the end of the digestive tract, is likely involved in waste elimination and responding to stimuli in the anal region.

questions.explanation: The nematode nervous system consists of a set of neuronal processes that run lengthwise on the nematode body. These processes consist of neurons that have a cell body also known as a neurocyte. A group of neurocytes is called a ganglion. These ganglion connect to the nerve ring which surrounds the Pharynx.

questions.text: Mollusks are soft-bodied, triploblastic animals, most of them are protected by a shell of calcium carbonate secreted by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Molluscs are triploblastic coelomates that exhibit bilateral symmetry. Most animals possess shells. The body is covered by a glandular epithelial envelope called a mantle which secretes a calcareous shell.
The outermost germ layer in triploblastic animals; forms the skin and nervous system but doesn't directly secrete the mollusk shell.
The middle germ layer is involved in forming muscles, connective tissues, and some organ systems; not responsible for shell secretion in mollusks.
The innermost germ layer contributes to the digestive tract and certain internal organs but is not associated with shell production in mollusks.

questions.explanation: In shelled molluses, the mantle is the organ that forms the shell. and adds to the shell to increase its size and strength as the animal grows. Shell material is secreted by the ectodermic (epithelial) cells of the mantle tissue.

questions.text: The internal opening of nephridia is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the external opening of nephridia, allowing excretory substances to be expelled from the body.
The correct answer. It is the internal opening of nephridia, serving as the entry point for waste and fluids to enter the excretory system.
A type of excretory organ found in some invertebrates, functioning similarly to nephridia by filtering waste products from the blood.
This term is not typically used in describing the openings of nephridia.

questions.explanation: Nephronstome is the internal opening of nephridia, serving as the entry point for waste and fluids to enter the excretory system.

questions.text: Locomotary organs are_in earthworms and_in Neries respectively.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Locomotary setae are for crawling and are the bristles that are visible on the exterior of the earthworm. Slow creeping movements of Neries are carried out by the action of parapodia only.
The organs of locomotion in annelids are chitinous chaetae or setae embedded in sacs (earthworms) or on. parapodia present in the body wall (e.g., Nereis). Chaetae are absent in Leech.
Locomotary setae are for crawling and are the bristles that are visible on the exterior of the earthworm. Slow creeping movements of Neries are carried out by the action of parapodia only.
Slow creeping movements of Neries are carried out by the action of parapodia only.

questions.explanation: Locomotary setae are for crawling and are the bristles that are visible on the exterior of the earthworm. Slow creeping movements of Neries are carried out by the action of parapodia only.

questions.text: The most successful group and the largest phylum of the animals:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A diverse group including snails, clams, and octopuses; is not the largest phylum, but still significant in terms of species diversity.
Comprises sponges; not the largest phylum, limited in species diversity compared to others.
Contains segmented worms like earthworms and leeches, diverse but not the largest phylum.

Phylum Arthropoda is a large group consisting of a great variety among them. The most successful and largest animal phylum is Phylum Arthropoda because of its adaptability, segmentation, exoskeleton, and enormous species diversity.

questions.explanation: Phylum Arthropoda is the most successful animal group, defined by its immense species diversity and versatile body plans.

questions.text: Natural silk is not:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Natural silk is primarily composed of fibrous proteins, specifically fibroin.
Silk is produced by specialized silk, not modified salivary glands, in insects like silkworms.
Natural silk is obtained from silkworms, which are insects.
Natural silk is not a glycolipid. It is a protein, specifically fibroin, produced by silkworms.

questions.explanation: Natural silk is fibrous protein produced by larvae of silk worm.

questions.text: Scavenger insects may eat:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Dead matter present in an organism
options.explanation: This can include dead cells or tissues within a living organism, but scavenger insects typically feed on external dead material.
Some insects are scavengers and they eat up dead animal and vegetable matter. Insect larvae are a source of food for fish.
While some insects feed exclusively on dead plant matter, scavenger insects aren't restricted to only plant sources.
Scavenger insects scavenge and feed on dead, decaying material rather than preying on live animals.

questions.explanation: Scavengers are those animals that feed on dead animals and plants e.g. Drosophila and Vulture.

questions.text: Members of this phylum are exclusively marine:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phylum containing sponges, which can be found in both marine and freshwater environments.
Includes jellyfish, corals, and sea anemones, most of which are marine, but some can also be found in freshwater.
This phylum encompasses diverse creatures like snails, clams, and octopuses, found in marine, freshwater, and terrestrial habitats.
There are over 5,000 known species of echinoderms. They are marine organisms living at the sea bottom.

questions.explanation: The echinoderms are exclusively marine.There are over 5,000 known species of echinoderms. They are marine organisms living at the sea bottom.

questions.text: The organs of locomotion in echinoderms are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This isn't a specific locomotion organ in echinoderms; they primarily use other structures for movement.
Typically seen in animals like squids and certain aquatic invertebrates, not a method used by echinoderms.
Found in certain segmented worms, not in echinoderms; these structures aid in movement but are not present in this phylum.
The motile species move with the help of tube feet. Each tube foot is a soft saclike structure present along the edges of grooves present in the arms.

questions.explanation: The motile species move with the help of tube feet. Each tube foot is a soft saclike structure present along the edges of grooves present in the arms.

questions.text: Water enters the water vascular system through:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Part of the water vascular system in echinoderms, but not the entry point for water into the system.
These are bulb-like structures connected to the tube feet and are involved in controlling their extension and retraction but not the entry point for water.
Openings in the heart of arthropods are not relevant to the water vascular system in echinoderms.
The water circulates through these channels. Water enters these canals through a sieve-like plate called madreporite present on the aboral body surface.

questions.explanation: The madreporite is a sieve-like, porous plate found on the aboral surface (the side opposite the mouth) of echinoderms such as starfish.It acts as the entry point for water into the water vascular system.Water enters through the madreporite and then circulates through a series of canals (stone canal → ring canal → radial canals → tube feet).

questions.text: Fate of the blastomeres is foretold in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Echinoderms exhibit radial cleavage, but the "fate" of blastomeres is not specifically foretold in this manner.
These organisms share some embryonic features with chordates but don't typically have a fate foretold through blastomeres in the same sense.
Arthropods show a spiral and determination cleavage and there fate of each blastomere is foretold.
Chordates, including vertebrates, exhibit various embryonic development patterns, but the fate of blastomeres in the same predictive manner as in arthropods isn't a defining characteristic.

questions.explanation: Arthropods are proterostomes in which cleavage is determinate (fate of blastomeres is foretold). Each blastomere developed into its special structure.

questions.text: Which one of the following is the primary host of liver fluke?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Humans can be infected by the liver fluke, but they are not the primary host.
Snails serve as an intermediate host in the life cycle of the liver fluke but not the primary host.
Fasciola (Liver luke): It is an endoparasite in sheep and occasionally in human beings.
Dogs can also be infected by the liver fluke, but they are not the primary host in the life cycle of the parasite.

questions.explanation: Sheep is the primary host of liver fluke.Fasciola (liver fluke) is a leaf-shaped, dorsoventrally flattened endoparasite belonging to phylum Platyhelminthes that lives in the liver and bile ducts of sheep and may accidentally infect humans. It feeds on blood and tissues, has an incomplete digestive system, and possesses oral and ventral suckers for attachment. Being hermaphrodite, it shows internal fertilization and has a complex digenetic life cycle involving a freshwater snail (Lymnaea) as the secondary host. Infection in humans occurs by eating water plants contaminated with its larval stage (metacercaria), leading to liver disease called fascioliasis.

questions.text: Which one of the following is an example of a free living carnivorous flatworm?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Liver flukes are an important cause of fascioliasis in grazing sheep and cattle. The primary host for liver fluke is sheep and intermediate host is snail.
Dugesia is a member of the phylum Planaria. Planarians are free-living flatworms and have a much simpler life history. They inhabit freshwater and are carnivores (even without teeth) or scavengers.
Tapeworms are flat, segmented worms that live in the intestines of some animals. Animals can become infected with these parasites when grazing in pastures or drinking contaminated water.
Schistosoma are trematodes whose complex life cycles require an intermediate freshwater snail host; they infect approximately 200 million people worldwide.

questions.explanation: Dugesia is a member of the phylum Planaria. Planarians are free-living flatworms and have a much simpler life history. They inhabit freshwater and are carnivores (even without teeth) or scavengers.

questions.text: Ascaris is which one of the following?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Parasites that live on the external surface of their host organisms. Ascaris is not an ectoparasite.
Ascaris primarily infests the intestines, not the respiratory tract.
Ascaris lumbricoides is an intestinal parasite of man.
Ascaris does not primarily infect the urinogenital tract; its main location is the intestines.

questions.explanation: Ascaris lumbricoides is an intestinal parasite of man.

questions.text: Polymorphism is a feature exhibited by members of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Polymorphism is the phenomenon where individuals within the species exhibit various functional and structural differences. Phylum coelenterata which shows polymorphism as individuals are sighting of it possessing modified polyp-like structures. It is particularly seen in its member Hydrozoa.
Arthropods are invertebrates with jointed legs. They make up about 75% of all animals on Earth and have a major role in maintaining ecosystems.
Poriferans are pore-bearing first multicellular animals. The pores are known as Ostia. The poriferans have a spongy appearance and are therefore called sponges.
The simplest animals that are bilaterally symmetrical and triploblastic (composed of three fundamental cell layers) are the Platyhelminthes, the flatworms.

questions.explanation: Polymorphism is the phenomenon where individuals within the species exhibit various functional and structural differences. Phylum coelenterata which shows polymorphism as individuals are sighting of it possessing modified polyp-like structures. It is particularly seen in its member Hydrozoa.

questions.text: _ is a triploblastic organism.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Jellyfish: This option is not the correct answer. Jellyfish belong to phylum Cnidaria. They are diploblastic and contain only two germ layers i.e, ectoderm and endoderm.
Sea Anemone: This option is not correct. Sea anemones belong to phylum Cnidaria. They are diploblastic and contain only two germ layers i.e, ectoderm and endoderm.

Tapeworm: This option is the correct answer. Tapeworms are triploblastic organisms. They belong to the phylum Platyhelminthes and have three germ layers: ectoderm, mesoderm and endoderm. They are considered triploblastic
Corals: This option is not the correct answer. Corals belong to phylum Cnidaria. They are diploblastic and contain only two germ layers i.e, ectoderm and endoderm.

questions.explanation: Tapeworms are triploblastic organisms. They belong to the phylum Platyhelminthes and have three germ layers: ectoderm, mesoderm and endoderm. They are considered triploblastic.

questions.text: In arthropods, the body cavity is in the form of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A true body cavity lined with mesoderm. Arthropods typically lack a coelom.
A body cavity partially lined with mesoderm. Arthropods generally don't have a pseudocoelom.
The coelom is not present as the main body cavity of Arthropoda. Instead, a haemocoel has developed.
The primitive body cavity or primitive gut, not directly related to the body cavity form in arthropods.

questions.explanation: In Arthropoda, the true coelom is greatly reduced and does not function as the main body cavity. Instead, they possess a large, blood-filled cavity called a haemocoel, which is part of an open circulatory system. In this haemocoel, tissues and organs are bathed directly in haemolymph (blood-like fluid), allowing the transport of nutrients and gases. Thus, the coelom is present only in small, reduced regions around certain organs, while the haemocoel serves as the primary body cavity in arthropods.

questions.text: _is also called liver fluke.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A free-living flatworm is commonly known as a planarian, not referred to as the liver fluke.

With few exceptions, the Platyhelminthes are parasites, mostly endoparasites, i.e., live inside their hosts. The most common examples are Taenia solium (tapeworm), Fasciola hepatica (liver luke), and Schistosoma (blood luke).
These are tapeworms, not liver flukes. They are segmented parasites found in the intestines of their hosts.
A marine invertebrate present at the bottom of the sea giving colorful appearance to the sea bed, not related to liver flukes.

questions.explanation: Fasciola hepatica, also known as the common liver fluke lives in bile duct.With few exceptions, the Platyhelminthes are parasites, mostly endoparasites, i.e., live inside their hosts. The most common examples are Taenia solium (tapeworm), Fasciola hepatica (liver luke), and Schistosoma (blood luke).

questions.text: Name of common gut roundworm parasite of humans and pigs.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ascaris lumbricoides is a common intestinal roundworm parasite found in humans (and a closely related species, Ascaris suum, infects pigs). These worms live in the lumen of the small intestine, usually in the jejunum or ileum. Infection occurs through ingestion of eggs from contaminated food or water. The larvae migrate through the lungs before returning to the intestine to mature. This is the correct answer.

Pheretima posthuma is a species of earthworm, commonly found in moist soil. It plays an important role in aerating the soil and decomposing organic matter. It is not parasitic and does not infect humans or pigs. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Lumbricus terrestris is another species of earthworm, also non-parasitic. It lives freely in soil and helps improve soil fertility by burrowing and digesting decaying material. It does not live in the human or pig intestine, so this is incorrect.

Hirudo medicinalis is the medicinal leech, a segmented annelid that feeds on the blood of vertebrates. It is used in medicine for therapeutic purposes but is not a gut roundworm parasite. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Ascaris worms inhabit the lumen of the small intestine, usually in the jejunum or ileus. They have a life span of 10 months to 2 years and then are passed in the stool.

questions.text: In radial symmetry, all body parts are arranged around the central axis. Radial symmetry represents _ mode of life.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Radial symmetry is a characteristic feature of animals like Jellyfish and Sea anemones of the phylum Cnidaria, which are sessile and bottom-dwelling.
Streamlining seems to be a design that provides the least amount of resistance to liquid flow. Which refers to motility, not sessile.
Radial symmetry, or any type of symmetry, has nothing to do with an active mode of life since it’s a physical feature, not an anatomical one.
Parasitic nature means dependence on another organism. Symmetry has nothing to do with an organism’s dependence; it’s only a physical characteristic.

questions.explanation: Radial symmetry is a basic body plane in which the organism can be divided into similar halves by passing a plane at any angle along a central axis. It is characteristic of sessile and bottom-dwelling animals, such as the sea anemone and starfish.

questions.text: Pseudo-coelomates have a body cavity but it is not true coelom. Which one of the following is included in the group?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Planaria belongs to the phylum Platyhelminthes (flatworms). These organisms are acoelomates, meaning they lack any body cavity between the body wall and digestive tract. Their internal organs are embedded in solid tissue called parenchyma. Therefore, Planaria is not a pseudocoelomate and this option is incorrect.

Earthworm belongs to the phylum Annelida. Members of this group are true coelomates (eucoelomates) because they have a body cavity (coelom) completely lined by mesodermal tissue. This allows for better organ development and movement. Hence, earthworm is not a pseudocoelomate, making this option incorrect.

Tapeworm belongs to the phylum Platyhelminthes, like Planaria. It is also an acoelomate, meaning it does not possess a body cavity at all. Its body is flattened and filled with tissue, with no true or false coelom. Thus, tapeworm is not a pseudocoelomate, so this option is incorrect.

Ascaris lumbricoides belongs to the phylum Nematoda (roundworms), which are pseudocoelomates. They possess a body cavity called a pseudocoel, which is not completely lined by mesoderm. This cavity serves as a hydrostatic skeleton and helps in the circulation of nutrients. Therefore, Ascaris is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Ascaris lumbricoides is an intestinal parasite of man that is included in pseudo-coelomates.

questions.text: Taenia is an endoparasite of humans, pigs, and cattle, which belongs to the phylum:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phylum containing jellyfish, corals, and sea anemones, are characterized by stinging cells called cnidocytes. Taenia is not part of this phylum.
This phylum includes segmented worms like earthworms and leeches. Taenia does not belong to this group.
A now outdated group that included roundworms like Ascaris. Taenia is not classified under this phylum.
With few exceptions the Platyhelminthes are parasites, mostly endoparasites, i.e., live inside their hosts. The most common examples are Taenia solium (tapeworm)

questions.explanation: With few exceptions the Platyhelminthes are parasites, mostly endoparasites, i.e., live inside their hosts. The most common examples are Taenia solium (tapeworm).

questions.text: The body of_ consists of segments called Proglottids, which contain mainly sex organs.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Flatworms with a simple body structure, lack segments like proglottids. They reproduce asexually through regeneration.
A type of parasitic flatworm (a fluke) that primarily infests the liver and doesn't have the segmented body structure seen in proglottids.
Ascaris is a roundworm, not a flatworm, and doesn't possess proglottids or segmented structures.

An endoparasite of humans, cattle and pig, that completes its life cycle in two hosts. The intermediate host is pig or cattle. The body is ribbon-like and divided into segments called proglottids which contain mainly sex organs.

questions.explanation: The major part of the tapeworm is called strobila and it consists of seginents, proglottids. They each contain both male and female reproductive organs.

questions.text: _is a common parasite of the intestine of humans and pigs, which belongs to phylum nematode.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is a tapeworm that typically infects the intestines of humans. It belongs to the phylum Platyhelminthes, not Nematoda.
This roundworm is a common parasite of the intestine in both humans and pigs, and it belongs to the phylum Nematoda.
These parasites, causing schistosomiasis, belong to the phylum Platyhelminthes. They are not nematodes but rather flukes.
This is a liver fluke, a parasite that infects the liver, and it belongs to the phylum Platyhelminthes, not Nematoda.

questions.explanation: Ascaris worms inhabit the lumen of the small intestine of human and pig.

questions.text: Snails are the intermediate hosts in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is an endoparasite in sheep and occasionally in human beings. It has suckers used for attachment to host tissue. It completes its life cycle in two hosts, a snail, sheep, or man. It lives in the bile duct of its hosts.
Snails serve as an intermediate host for different species of Schistosoma. They play a crucial role in the life cycle of these parasitic flatworms.
Pigs are the intermediate hosts for Taenia solium, not snails.
The larvae of Ancylostoma duodenale, a hookworm, typically develop in the soil and can infect humans through skin contact. They don't utilize snails as intermediate hosts.

questions.explanation: It is an endoparasite in sheep and occasionally in human beings. It has suckers used for attachment to host tissue. It completes its life cycle in two hosts, a snail, sheep, or man. It lives in the bile duct of its hosts.

questions.text: _is an intestinal parasite of man belonging to phylum Nematoda.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is a tapeworm that commonly infests the intestine, but it belongs to the phylum Platyhelminthes, not Nematoda.
Ascaris lumbricoides is an intestinal parasite of man included in nematoda.
There is no recognized parasite or term by this name.
This parasite is not a nematode; it's a blood fluke, belonging to the phylum Platyhelminthes.

questions.explanation: Ascaris lumbricoides worms inhabit the lumen of the small intestine of human and pig.Ascaris lumbricoides is an intestinal parasite of man included in nematoda.

questions.text: Following group is the example of acoelomates:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Annelids, such as earthworms and leeches, are true coelomates (eucoelomates). They possess a well-developed coelom, a fluid-filled cavity completely lined by mesoderm. This cavity allows the free movement and growth of internal organs. Therefore, annelids are not acoelomates, making this option incorrect.

Aschelminthes (or Nematoda) are pseudocoelomates, meaning they have a body cavity called a pseudocoel, which is not completely lined by mesoderm. This cavity serves as a hydrostatic skeleton. Since they do have a body cavity (though false), they are not acoelomates, so this option is incorrect.

Molluscs, including snails, clams, and octopuses, are true coelomates. They have a well-developed coelomic cavity that allows complex organ systems to function efficiently. Thus, they cannot be classified as acoelomates. This option is incorrect.

Platyhelminthes, or flatworms, are acoelomates, meaning they completely lack a body cavity. Their internal organs are embedded in a solid mass of mesenchyme between the body wall and digestive tract. Therefore, Platyhelminthes is the correct answer and a classic example of acoelomates.

questions.explanation: Acoelomates do not possess a body cavity or coelom i.e the fluid-filled cavity between the digestive tract and the body wall that also surrounds the other internal organs is absent. Platyhelminthes and sponges are classic examples of acoelomates. In some animals, the embryonic blastocoel acts as a fluid-filled cavity. This body cavity is partially lined with the mesodermal derivative cells. Here, the coelom is much reduced or lost. It is not considered a true coelom, and hence the name pseudocoelomates. Examples include nematodes (roundworms). Animals that have their body cavities filled with fluid are called coelomates. Their internal organs are suspended in the fluid cavity. Examples include arthropods, annelids, molluscs, hemichordates, echinoderms and chordates.

questions.text: The larva formed during the life cycle of annelida is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glochidium is the larva of freshwater mussels, belonging to the phylum Mollusca, not Annelida. It develops as an external parasite on fish.
A bipinnaria is a larval form found in echinoderms, specifically starfish, and not associated with annelids.
Trochophore is a small, translucent, free-swimming larva characteristic of marine annelids and some mollusks, making it the correct answer for annelids.
A tornaria is a larval form seen in Hemichordata, such as acorn worms, and is not related to annelids.

questions.explanation: The trochophore larva is a distinctive stage in the life cycle of marine annelids and is also seen in some mollusks. This larva is free-swimming and characterized by its translucent body and ciliary bands that aid in locomotion. The other options, such as glochidium, bipinnaria, and tornaria, belong to other phyla—Mollusca, Echinodermata, and Hemichordata, respectively—and are not associated with Annelida.

questions.text: Platyhelminthes are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Organisms with two germ layers are diploblastic, and bilateral symmetry refers to a body plan where the right and left sides mirror each other. However, Platyhelminthes have three germ layers, so this option doesn't accurately describe them.

The Platyhelminthes are triploblastic acoelomates. There is development of a third layer, then mesoderm, which separates the ectoderm and endoderm. The Platyhelminthes exhibit bilateral symmetry, and body is unsegmented.
Radial symmetry involves body parts arranged around a central point, which doesn't apply to Platyhelminthes, and they are triploblastic, not radially symmetric.
Platyhelminthes are bilaterally symmetric and triploblastic, not radially symmetric or diploblastic.

questions.explanation: Platyhelminthes have the following important characteristics: They are triploblastic, acoelomate, and bilaterally symmetrical. They may be free-living or parasites.The body has a soft covering with or without cilia.Their body is dorsoventrally flattened without any segments and appears like a leaf.

questions.text: Which group of organisms has the following features?1. Three pairs of jointed legs2. Three-part segmented body3. One pair of antennae
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Arachnids have four pairs of legs, not three, and their body is divided into two segments - the cephalothorax and abdomen.
Crustaceans typically have a varying number of legs, often more than three pairs and their bodies can have multiple segments.

In insects, there is a pair of antennae and compound eyes on the head. insects have three distinct regions head, thorax, and abdomen. The thorax has three segments in which are present three pairs of jointed legs and in many one or two pairs of wings.
Myriapods, like centipedes and millipedes, have numerous pairs of legs and a segmented body, but they do not possess just three pairs of legs or a three-part body plan.

questions.explanation: In insects, the head bears a pair of antennae, which serve as important sensory organs for detecting smell, touch, and vibrations, and also carries compound eyes, each made up of many small visual units that provide a wide field of view. The insect body is divided into three distinct regions: head, thorax, and abdomen. The thorax, which is the locomotory center, consists of three segments prothorax, mesothorax, and metathorax. Each of these segments carries one pair of jointed legs, giving insects a total of three pairs of legs. Additionally, many insects bear one or two pairs of wings on the middle and last thoracic segments, making them the only invertebrates capable of active flight. The abdomen contains major internal organs, including those for digestion, excretion, and reproduction, completing the characteristic body plan of insects.

questions.text: Platyhelminthes means:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Platyhelminthes is a phylum of invertebrates that includes flatworms. "Platy" means flat, and "helminths" refers to worms. Therefore, "Platyhelminthes" directly translates to "flatworms."
Roundworms belong to the phylum Nematoda, which is a distinct group from Platyhelminthes. Roundworms have a cylindrical, round body, unlike the flat body of flatworms.
Segmented worms belong to the phylum Annelida, characterized by their body divisions into segments, unlike the largely unsegmented flatworms.
The term "Platyhelminthes" does have a specific meaning, referring to a group of flatworms, so this isn't the correct option.

questions.explanation: Word Platyhelminthes (derived from the Greek platy, meaning "flat" while helminth meaning "worm") are a phylum of relatively simple bilaterian, unsegmented, soft-bodied invertebrates.

questions.text: Reptiles flourished throughout_era.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The Cenozoic era began around 66 million years ago and continues to the present.
The Devonian period occurred in the Paleozoic era, around 419 to 359 million years ago.
The Proterozoic eon was an ancient geological time extending from around 2.5 billion to 541 million years ago.
Reptiles flourished throughout Mesozoic era (225-65 million years).

questions.explanation: Reptiles flourished throughout the Mesozoic era (225-65 million years ago).

questions.text: Birds do not have teeth so the function of teeth is performed by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Birds use their beaks for a variety of tasks like grasping and eating food. However, the beak is not specialized for grinding food like teeth.
The pharynx is involved in the passage of food and air but does not perform any grinding or crushing functions.
The stomach is essential for digestion using acids and enzymes, but it does not mechanically grind food like teeth do.
The gizzard is a specialized, muscular part of the bird's digestive system that grinds and crushes food, effectively substituting the function of teeth.

questions.explanation: The gizzard is a specialized, muscular part of the bird's digestive system that grinds and crushes food, effectively substituting the function of teeth.

questions.text: Which of them is not poikilotherm?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Spiny anteaters(Echidnas) are mammals and, like most mammals, are endothermic, meaning they can regulate their body temperature internally. They are not poikilothermic (cold-blooded).
Lizards are ectothermic, or poikilothermic, meaning their body temperature fluctuates with the surrounding environment.
Crocodiles are also ectothermic, or poikilothermic, relying on external sources to regulate their body temperature.
Snakes are ectothermic animals, depending on external sources to control their body temperature. They are poikilothermic.

questions.explanation: A poikilotherm is an animal whose internal temperature varies considerably. It is the opposite of a homeotherm, an animal which maintains thennal homeostasis. Spiny ant eaters belong to homeotherm.

questions.text: The lower jaw is composed of only one large bone:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Reptiles have lower jaws composed of multiple bones, not just one large bone.
Amphibians also have lower jaws made up of several bones, not a single large bone.
Fish belonging to this class, such as sharks and rays, have lower jaws that are indeed made of cartilage, but they are not a single large bone.
The lower jaw in mammals is composed of only one large bone and articulates directly with skull.

questions.explanation: The lower Jaw of mammals consists of only one bone, the dentary, and the jaw hinge connects the dentary to the squamosal (flat) part of the temporal bone in the skull. Working together, these muscles permit up-and-down and side-to-side movements of the jaw,making chewing possible which is unique to mammals.

questions.text: Opossum and koala bear belongs to the sub-class:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This subclass includes the egg-laying mammals, such as the platypus and echidna.
Eutherian mammals are placental mammals, which give birth to relatively developed young ones.
This subclass comprises marsupial mammals, like opossums and koalas, which give birth to relatively undeveloped young that further develop in a pouch.
Monotremes are egg-laying mammals, such as the platypus and echidna.
